item.content:encoded:
Mockitožóź ŽĆÄŪēīžĄú ž†ēŽ¶¨ŪēėÍłį žúĄŪēī žěźŽ£ĆŽ•ľ Í≤ÄžÉČŪēėŽćė ž§Ď, Ž™® ŽłĒŽ°úÍĪįžĚė test doubležóź ÍīÄŪēú ÍłÄžóźžĄú ÍįÄžßú ÍįĚž≤īŽ•ľ StubžĚīŽĚľ Ž∂ÄŽ•īŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚĄ ŽįúÍ≤¨ŪĖąŽč§. ÍįÄžßú ÍįĚž≤īŽĚľÍ≥§ Mock ŽįĖžóź Ž™įŽĚľžĄú ž†ĄŽ∂Ä MockžĚīŽĚľÍ≥† žÉĚÍįĀŪēėÍ≥† žěąžóąŽäĒŽćį, žôú StubžĚīŽĚľŽäĒ Žč®žĖīŽ•ľ žā¨žö©ŪĖąŽäĒžßÄ Í∂ĀÍłąŪēīž†łžĄú ž°įžā¨ŪēīŽī§Žč§.

Test double

Test double(ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠)žĚÄ žėĀŪôĒ žÜć ŽįįžöįŽ•ľ ŽĆÄžč†ŪēėŽäĒ Stunt double(žä§ŪĄīŪäł ŽĆÄžó≠)žóźžĄú žú†ŽěėŽźėžóąžúľŽ©į, ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽ•ľ žßĄŪĖČŪēėÍłį žĖīŽ†§žöī Í≤Ĺžöį žĚīŽ•ľ ŽĆÄžč†Ūēī žßĄŪĖČŪēī ž£ľŽäĒ ÍįĚž≤īŽ•ľ ŽúĽŪēúŽč§. Žč§Ž•ł Žį©žčĚžúľŽ°ú žĄ§Ž™ÖŪēėžěźŽ©ī ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžÉĀžóź žßĎž§ĎŪē† žąė žěąŽŹĄŽ°Ě, žĚėž°īŪēėŽäĒ ÍĶ¨žĄĪžöĒžÜĆŽ•ľ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäłžóź žö©žĚīŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě Žč®žąúŪôĒŪēú ÍįĚž≤īŽ•ľ ŽúĽŪēúŽč§.

žó¨ÍłįžĄú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžÉĀžĚĄ System Under Test ž§Ąžó¨žĄú SUTŽĚľÍ≥† Ž∂ÄŽ•īÍ≥†, SUTÍįÄ žĚėž°īŪēėŽäĒ ÍĶ¨žĄĪžöĒžÜĆŽ•ľ Depended-On Component ž§Ąžó¨žĄú DOCŽĚľÍ≥† Ž∂ÄŽ•łŽč§.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/423bc43e-e3d8-4dc0-94dd-b01df5292d9f.webp" alt="Žč®žúĄ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłžóźžĄú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžÉĀ Í≥†Ž¶Ĺ"}

SUTžôÄ DOC ÍįĄžĚė ŪėĎŽ†•žĚī žĖīžÉČŪēėžßÄ žēäŽč§Ž©ī, ŪēīŽčĻ ÍłįŽä•(ŪĖČŽŹô)žĚĄ ŪēėŽāėžĚė Žč®žúĄŽ°ú Ž≥īÍ≥† Ūē®ÍĽė ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŪēėŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚĄ Sociable testŽĚľ ŪēúŽč§. Sociable testžĚė Í≤Ĺžöį, ŪėĄžě¨ žčúÍįĄÍ≥ľ ÍįôžĚī SUTÍįÄ ž†úžĖīŪē† žąė žóÜŽäĒ žÉĀŪô©žĚīŽāė Emailž≤ėŽüľ ŽĻĄžö©žĚī ŽßéžĚī ŽďúŽäĒ žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŪēėÍłį žĖīŽ†ĶŽč§.

žĚīžôÄ ŽįėŽĆÄŽ°ú, SUTžôÄ DOCžĚė žĚėž°īžĄĪžĚĄ ŽĀäÍ≥† ŪēėŽāėžĚė Ž™®Žďą Žč®žúĄŽ°ú ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŪēėŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚĄ Solitary testŽĚľ ŪēúŽč§. Solitary testžĚė Í≤Ĺžöį DOCŽ•ľ Žč®žąúŪēėÍ≥† ŪÜĶž†úŪē† žąė žěąŽäĒ ÍįĚž≤īŽ°ú ŽßĆŽď§žĖī SUTÍįÄ žôłŽ∂Ä žĖīŽĖ†Ūēú žöĒžĚłžóźŽŹĄ žėĀŪĖ•žĚĄ ŽįõžßÄ žēäŽŹĄŽ°Ě Í≥†Ž¶Ĺžčúžľú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł Ūē† žąė žěąŽč§. žĚīŽēĆ ŽßĆŽďúŽäĒ DOCÍįÄ ŽįĒŽ°ú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚīŽč§.

::v-alert ūüí° ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚė ŪēĄžöĒžĄĪžóź ŽĆÄŪēīžĄú žĄúžą†ŪēėŽč§Ž≥īŽčą sociable testÍįÄ Ž∂Äž†ēž†ĀžúľŽ°ú ŪĎúŪėĄŽźźŽäĒŽćį, žč§ž†úŽ°úŽäĒ Í∑łŽ†ážßÄ žēäŽč§. ž∂ĒŪõĄžóź Solitary testžôÄ Sociable testžĚė žě•Žč®ž†źžĚĄ ž†ēŽ¶¨Ūēī Ž≥īŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŪēėÍ≤†Žč§. ::

žĖłž†ú žā¨žö©ŪēėŽäĒÍįÄ?

  • ž†úžĖīŪē† žąė žóÜŽäĒ žÉĀŪô©žĚĄ ŪÜĶž†úŪē† ŽēĆ.

ŽěúŽć§ žąęžěź, ŪėĄžě¨ žčúÍįĄ, DBžóźžĄú ŪäĻž†ē Ž†ąžĹĒŽďú žĚĹÍłįŽäĒ Ūē≠žÉĀ ÍįôžĚÄ Í≤įÍ≥ľŽ•ľ Ž≥īžě•ŪēėžßÄ žēäŽäĒŽč§. ŽėźŪēú ŽĄ§ŪäłžõĆŪĀ¨ žė§Ž•ėŽāė žôłŽ∂Ä žĄúŽĻĄžä§žĚė žÉĀŪÉúÍįÄ Ūē≠žÉĀ ž†ēžÉĀžĚī žēĄŽčź žąėŽŹĄ žěąŽč§. žĚīž≤ėŽüľ SUTÍįÄ ž†úžĖīŪē† žąė žóÜŽäĒ žÉĀŪô©žĚĄ žĚľÍīÄŽźú žÉĀŪô©žúľŽ°ú ŪÜĶž†úŪē† ŽēĆ žā¨žö©Ūē† žąė žěąŽč§.

  • ŽĻĄžö©žĚī ŽßéžĚī ŽďúŽäĒ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽ•ľ Ūē† ŽēĆ.

žĚīŽ©ĒžĚľ, SMS ŽėźŽäĒ Ž©Ēžč†ž†Ä ŽďĪžúľŽ°ú Ž≥īŽāīŽäĒ ŪÖúŪĒĆŽ¶ŅžĚīŽāė Ž©ĒžčúžßÄŪĀźžóź Ž≥īŽāīŽäĒ Ž©ĒžčúžßÄ Žāīžö© ŽďĪžĚÄ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĻĄžö©žĚī ŽßéžĚī Žď§žĖī ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŪēėÍłį žĖīŽ†§žöī ÍłįŽä•žĚīŽč§. žĚī Í≤Ĺžöį, Í≤Äž¶Ěžóź ŪēĄžöĒŪēú Žāīžö©žĚĄ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žóź ž†Äžě•Ūēú Ží§ Í≤Äž¶ĚŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

  • ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žěĎžĄĪžĚĄ žúĄŪēú ŪôėÍ≤Ĺ ÍĶ¨ž∂ēŪē† ŽēĆ.

ŪäĻž†ē ÍłįŽä•žĚī žēĄžßĀ ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŽźėžßÄ žēäžēĄ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽ•ľ Ūē† žąė žóÜŽäĒ Í≤Ĺžöį žā¨žö©Ūē† žąė žěąŽč§.

Test doubležĚė žĘÖŽ•ė

Dummy

ÍįÄžě• ÍłįŽ≥łž†ĀžĚł ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žúľŽ°ú, ŪĆĆŽĚľŽĮłŪĄį ÍįížúľŽ°ú ŽĄėÍłłŽēĆŽßĆ žā¨žö©ŽźėŽ©į žč§ž†úŽ°ú žďįžĚīžßÄŽäĒ žēäŽäĒ ÍįĚž≤īžĚīŽč§. ŪēĄŽďúžóź ÍłįŽ≥łÍįíŽßĆ žĪĄžõĆž†łžěąŽäĒ ÍįĚž≤īŽāė Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďú ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŽ∂ÄÍįÄ ŽĻĄžĖīžěąŽäĒ ÍįĚž≤īÍįÄ žĚīžóź ŪēīŽčĻŽźúŽč§.

@Test
void dummyTest() {
    Article dummyArticle = new Article("", "", new Date(), 0);

    assertThrows(Exception.class, () -> articleService.register(dummyArticle));
}

class ConsolePrinter {
    public void print(String text){
        System.out.println(text);
    }
}

class DummyConsolePrinter extends ConsolePrinter {
    @Override
    public void print(String text) {}
}

Stub

DummyžóźžĄú Ūēú Žč®Í≥Ą žóÖÍ∑łŽ†ąžĚīŽďú Žźú ÍįĚž≤īžĚīŽ©į ŪēėŽďú žĹĒŽĒ©Žźú ÍįížĚĄ ŽįėŪôėŪēúŽč§. ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ž§Ď ŽįúžÉĚŪēėŽäĒ Ūėłž∂úžóź ŽĆÄŪēīžĄú ŽĮłŽ¶¨ Í≤įÍ≥ľŽ•ľ ž§ÄŽĻĄŪēīŽÜďŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚÄ StubbingžĚīŽĚľ ŪēúŽč§.

class StubArticleRepository implements ArticleRepository {

    @Override
    public List<Article> getAll() {
        return List.of(
                new Article("title_1", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2020, 5, 19, 10, 50), 1),
                new Article("title_2", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2021, 8, 23, 10, 50), 0)
        );
    }
}

Spy

žč§ž†ú ÍįĚž≤īžôÄ ŽŹôžĚľŪēú ŽŹôžěĎžĚĄ Ūē† žąėŽŹĄ žěąÍ≥† StubžúľŽ°úžć® ŽŹôžěĎŪē† žąėŽŹĄ žěąŽč§. Ūėłž∂úžĚī ŽźėžóąžĚĄ ŽēĆ, ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł Í≤Äž¶Ěžóź ŪēĄžöĒŪēú ŽćįžĚīŪĄįŽ•ľ ž†Äžě•ŪēúŽč§.

class TestDoubleTest {

    @Test
    void spyTest() {
        SpyArticleRepository spy = new SpyArticleRepository();
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(spy);
        Article stub1 = new Article("title_1", "content_1", LocalDateTime.of(2020, 5, 19, 10, 50), 1);
        Article stub2 = new Article("title_2", "content_2", LocalDateTime.of(2021, 6, 29, 11, 15), 2);

        Long id1 = service.register(stub1);
        Long id2 = service.register(stub2);

        assertEquals(1, id1);
        assertEquals(2, id2);
        assertEquals(2, spy.getArticles().size());      // Spy's save behavior happened 2 times

    }
}

class SpyArticleRepository implements ArticleRepository {

    private List<Article> articles = new ArrayList<>();

    @Override
    public Long save(Article article) {
        articles.add(article);
        return 1L;
    }

    public List<Article> getArticles() {
        return articles;
    }
}

class ArticleService {
    private ArticleRepository repository;

    public ArticleService(ArticleRepository repository) {
        this.repository = repository;
    }

    public Long register(Article article) {
        return repository.save(article);
    }
}

Fake

žč§ž†ú ÍįĚž≤īžĚė ŽŹôžěĎžĚĄ Žč®žąúŪôĒŪēīžĄú ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŪēú ÍįĚž≤īžĚīŽ©į, ŽŹôž†ĀžúľŽ°ú ŽŹôžěĎžĚÄ ŪēėžßÄŽßĆ ŪĒĄŽ°úŽćēžÖė ŪôėÍ≤ĹžóźžĄú žā¨žö©ŪēėÍłįŽäĒ ž†ĀŪē©ŪēėžßÄ žēäŽč§. HashMapžĚĄ žĚīžö©ŪēīžĄú žĚłŽ©ĒŽ™®Ž¶¨ DBŽ•ľ ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŪēú Í≤ÉžĚĄ žėąŽ°ú Žď§ žąė žěąŽč§.

class ArticleRepositoryFake implements ArticleRepository {

    private Map<Long, Article> articles = new HashMap<>();

    @Override
    public Long save(Article article) {
        Long existId = article.getId();
        if (existId == null) {
            return createNewArticle(article);
        }
        updateOldArticle(existId, article);
        return existId;
    }

    private void updateOldArticle(Long existId, Article article) {
        articles.put(existId, article);
    }

    private Long createNewArticle(Article article) {
        Long id = (long) (getAll().size() + 1);
        this.articles.put(id, article);
        return id;
    }

}

Mock

Mock ŽĚľžĚīŽłĆŽü¨Ž¶¨Ž•ľ ŪÜĶŪēī ŽŹôž†ĀžúľŽ°ú žÉĚžĄĪŪēú ÍįĚž≤īžĚīŽč§. ÍĶ¨žĄĪŪēėÍłįžóź ŽĒįŽĚľ Dummy, Stub ŽėźŽäĒ Spyž≤ėŽüľ ŽŹôžěĎŪēúŽč§.

::v-alert ūüí° Mock ŽĚľžĚīŽłĆŽü¨Ž¶¨ ŽėźŽäĒ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžõĆŪĀ¨Ž•ľ žĚīžö©ŪēīžĄú Spy ÍįĚž≤ī žÉĚžĄĪŪēėŽ©ī, Ž≥īŪÜĶžĚÄ žč§ž†ú ÍįĚž≤īž≤ėŽüľ ŽŹôžěĎŪēėŽ©į žõźŪēėŽäĒ Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďúŽ•ľ žĄ†ŪÉĚž†ĀžúľŽ°ú Stubbing ŪēīžĄú žā¨žö©Ūē† žąė žěąŽč§. ŪēĄžěźÍįÄ ÍīÄžč¨žĚĄ ŽĎźÍ≥† žěąŽäĒ Mockito, Mockk, Spock Í∑łŽ¶¨Í≥† JestžóźžĄú ŪôēžĚłŪĖąžúľŽāė, ŪėĻžčú Žč§Ž•ł Í≤ĹžöįÍįÄ žěąŽč§Ž©ī ž†úŽ≥ī ŽįĒŽěÄŽč§. ::

@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)
class TestDoubleTest {

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a dummy")
    void mockTest1(@Mock Member dummyMember) {
        Article article = new Article("Title 1", "Content 1", now(), 10, dummyMember);
        ArticleRepository repository = new ArticleRepository();

        assertDoesNotThrow(() -> repository.save(article));
    }

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a stub")
    void mockTest2(@Mock ArticleRepository stubRepository) {
        when(stubRepository.findById(anyLong())).thenReturn(new Article("Title 1", "Content 1", now(), 10, new Member("","")));
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(stubRepository);

        Article article = service.fetch(1L);

        assertEquals("Title 1", article.getTitle());

    }

    @Spy
    ArticleRepository spyRepository;

    @Test
    @DisplayName("A mock behave like a spy")
    void mockTest3() {
        ArticleService service = new ArticleService(spyRepository);

        service.fetch(1L);
        service.fetch(2L);

        verify(spyRepository, times(2)).findById(anyLong());

    }
}

ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚė ŪēúÍ≥Ą

  • Ž¶¨ŪĆ©ŪÜ†Ž¶¨ ŽāīžĄĪžĚī ŽāģŽč§.

Ž¶¨ŪĆ©ŪÜ†Ž¶¨ ŽāīžĄĪžĚī ŽāģŽč§ŽäĒ ŽßźžĚī žôÄŽčŅžßÄ žēäžĚĄ žąė žěąŽäĒŽćį, ÍīÄŽ¶¨ ŽĻĄžö©žĚī ž¶ĚÍįÄŪēúŽč§ŽäĒ Í≤ÉÍ≥ľ ÍįôžĚÄ ŽßźžĚīŽč§. žėąž†úŽ•ľ ŪÜĶŪēī ŪôēžĚłŪēīŽ≥īžěź. ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚĄ žĚīžö©ŪēėŽ©ī žēĄŽěėž≤ėŽüľ SUTžĚė ŽāīŽ∂Ä ÍĶ¨ŪėĄžĚī žôłŽ∂Äžóź ŽďúŽü¨ŽāėÍ≤Ć ŽźúŽč§.

class SutServiceTest {

  @Test
  public void someMethodTest() {
    // given
    FooService mockFooService = mock(FooService.class);
    SutService sut = new SutService(mockFooService);

    // when
    when(mockFooService.bar()).thenReturn(1); // Internal implementation was exposed
    sut.someMethod();
    ...

    // then
    verify(mockFooService).bar();             // Internal implementation was exposed
    ...
  }
}

class SutService {
  private FooService fooService;

  public SutService(FooService fooService) {
    this.fooService = fooService;
  }

  public void someMethod() {
    fooService.bar();
  }
}

interface FooService{
  int bar();
}

žĚīŽēĆ DOCžĚė Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďú žĚīŽ¶ĄžĚīŽāė ŪĆĆŽĚľŽĮłŪĄįÍįÄ Ž≥ÄÍ≤ĹŽź† Í≤Ĺžöį, Žč§žčú ŽßźŪēī Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďú žčúÍ∑łŽčąž≤ėÍįÄ Ž≥ÄÍ≤ĹŽź† Í≤Ĺžöį ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĹĒŽďúŽŹĄ ÍįôžĚī žąėž†ēŽźėžĖīžēľ ŪēúŽč§.

class RefactoringTest {

  @Test
  public void someMethodTest() {
    // given
    FooService mockFooService = mock(FooService.class);
    SutService sut = new SutService(mockFooService);

    // when
    when(mockFooService.bar()).thenReturn(1); // Break
    sut.someMethod();
    ...

    // then
    verify(mockFooService).bar();             // Break
    ...
  }
}

interface FooService{
  Bar bar(Factor factor);
}

žßĀž†Ď ŽßĆŽďúŽäĒ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚė Í≤ĹžöįžóźŽäĒ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žôłŽ∂ÄžóźžĄú StubbingžĚĄ ŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź ž§ĎŽ≥ĶžĚī ž§ĄžĖī Í∑ł ž†ēŽŹĄÍįÄ ŽćúŪēėŽč§. Í∑łŽü¨Žāė Mock ŽĚľžĚīŽłĆŽü¨Ž¶¨Ž•ľ žĚīžö©ŪēėŽäĒ Í≤Ĺžöį, ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽāīŽ∂ÄžóźžĄú StubbingžĚĄ ŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź Ž™®Žď† ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽ•ľ žąėž†ēŪēīžēľ ŪēėŽäĒ Í≥†ŪÜĶžĚĄ Í≤™Í≤Ć ŽźúŽč§.

  • žč†ŽĘįŽŹĄÍįÄ ŽĖ®žĖīžßĄŽč§.

StubbingžĚĄ žěĎžĄĪŪē† ŽēĆ, productionžóźžĄú DOCÍįÄ žč§ž†ú Í∑łŽ†áÍ≤Ć ŽŹôžěĎŪēėÍłł ÍłįŽĆÄŪēėŽ©īžĄú žěĎžĄĪŪēúŽč§. ŪēėžßÄŽßĆ ŽßČžÉĀ žč§ž†ú ÍįĚž≤īŽäĒ žėąžÉĀŪēúŽĆÄŽ°ú ŽŹôžěĎŪēėžßÄ žēäžĚĄ žąė žěąŽč§.

  • žĹĒŽďúŽüČžĚī žßÄŽāėžĻėÍ≤Ć ŽßéžēĄžßą žąė žěąŽč§.

"Ž¶¨ŪĆ©ŪÜ†Ž¶¨ ŽāīžĄĪžĚī ŽāģŽč§." žĚė ŽĀĚžóźžĄú žĖłÍłČŪēú žĚīžú†Ž°ú žĹĒŽďúŽüČžĚī žßÄŽāėžĻėÍ≤Ć ŽßéžēĄžßą žąė žěąŽč§.

Í≤įŽ°†

ÍįĀ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚĄ ÍĶ¨Ž∂ĄŪēėÍłį žČĹÍ≤Ć žĶúŽĆÄŪēú ÍįĄŽč®ŪēėÍ≤Ć ž†ēŽ¶¨ŪĖąžßÄŽßĆ, žč§ž†ú ŪĒĄŽ°úž†ĚŪäłŽ•ľ žßĄŪĖČŪēėŽč§Ž≥īŽ©ī ÍĶ¨Ž∂ĄŪěą Ž™ÖŪôēŪěą ŽźėžßÄ žēäžĚĄ žąė žěąŽč§. žĚīŽäĒ žā¨žč§ Í∑łŽ¶¨ žĚīžÉĀŪēú žĚľžĚī žēĄŽčĆŽćį, MSDN Ž©ĒÍĪįžßĄžóźžĄúŽäĒ žēĄŽěėžĚė Í∑łŽ¶ľžĚĄ žėąžčúŽ°ú Žď§Ž©į žĚīŽ°†Í≥ľ Žč¨Ž¶¨ žč§ž†úŽ°úŽäĒ ÍįĀ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚė žį®žĚīž†źžĚī Ž™®ŪėłŪēėŽč§Í≥† žĖėÍłįŪēúŽč§.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/6359cd30-db10-4487-bb72-fa8747daf75e.webp" alt="ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŽĆÄžó≠žĚė žä§ŪéôŪäłŽüľ"}

žö©žĖīŽ≥īŽč§ŽäĒ ÍįĚž≤īžĚė žó≠Ūē†žóź žßĎž§ĎŪēėŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚī ŽćĒ žÉĚžāįž†ĀžĚľ Í≤É ÍįôŽč§.

Reference

  • Mocks Aren't Stubs
  • Exploring The Continuum Of Test Doubles
  • What's the difference between a mock & stub?
  • Stub: Mocking Object For Tests In Yuk Recycle
  • xUnit Patterns
  • Test Double
  • Mock ÍįĚž≤ī Žā®žö©žĚÄ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ ŽßĚžĻúŽč§.

item.title: Starting unit test with JUnit 5 Part 2
item.description: In this article, there is no problem in testing even if you don't know it, but if you know it, you will organize the information that helps you tes...
item.content:encoded:
In this article, there is no problem in testing even if you don't know it, but if you know it, you will organize the information that helps you test it easily.

@ParameterizedTest

It is used to repeat the test several times using different arguments. Used in conjunction with the source annotation that specifies the value of the argument to be repeated. There are multiple source annotations, but knowing @ValueSource, @CsvSource is big enough.

@ValueSource

Specifies a single array of literal values as an argument. The supported types are short, byte, char, int, long, float, double, boolean, String and Class

@ParameterizedTest
@ValueSource(ints = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5})
void parameterizedTestExample(int candidate) {
    assertTrue(candidate < 10);
}

@CsvSource

Specifies comma-separated values as arguments. Each argument represents a CSV record.

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvSource({"ninja,700", "assassin,850", "'happy time',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" })
void csvSourceExample(String name, int score) { }

[1] ninja, 700
[2] assassin, 850
[3] happy time, 1_100
[4] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

::v-alert ūüí° From here on, I will omit the unnecessary parts so that I can focus on the changed attribute values of @CsvSource. ::

The useHeadersInDisplayName property allows the first record to be optionally used as a CSV header. If set to true, the first record is recognized as a header, skipped, and displayed as Display name.

@CsvSource(
    value = {"NAME,SCORE", "'happy time',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F"},
    useHeadersInDisplayName = true
)

[1] NAME = happy time, SCORE = 1_100
[2] NAME = @user-01x0w, SCORE = 0xE3F

The default delimiter is a comma (','). Char type delimiter can be specified through the delimiter attribute, and String type delimiter can be specified through the delimiterString attribute. Using the two properties together results in PreconditionViolationException.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "'happy time':1_100", "@user-01x0w:0xE3F" }, 
    delimiter = ':'
)

[1] happy time, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

Use the default quotation marks (') as quotation marks. An empty string enclosed in quotation marks returns an empty string unless you set a separate value for the emptyValue attribute.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "\"happy time\",1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
    quoteCharacter = '\"'
)

[1] happy time, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

```java[empty string] @CsvSource({"'',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" })

```[Output]
[1] , 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvSource(
    value = { "'',1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, 
    emptyValue = "{}"
)

[1] {}, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

The string specified in the nullValues property is replaced with null.

@CsvSource(
    value = { "NIL,1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" },
    nullValues = "NIL"
)

[1] null, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

If ignoreLeadingAndTrailingWhitespace = false is set, the front and back spaces are left intact.

::v-alert ūüí° The name attribute will be described in the Customizing display name section. ::

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[" + INDEX_PLACEHOLDER + "] ''{0}'', {1}")
@CsvSource(
        value = { "' happy time ',1_100", "@user-01x0w ,0xE3F" },
        ignoreLeadingAndTrailingWhitespace = false
)

[1] ' happy time', 1_100
[2] '@user-01x0w ', 0xE3F

A column with no value returns null.

```java[no value] @CsvSource( value = { ",1_100", "@user-01x0w,0xE3F" }, )

```[Output]
[1] null, 1_100
[2] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

In Java 15 and later, textBlock properties can be used to improve readability. All rows beginning with the # symbol are treated as annotations and must begin without leading spaces.

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvSource(delimiter = '|', quoteCharacter = '"', textBlock = """
#-----------------------------
#     NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100
#-----------------------------
  @user-01x0w  |    0xE3F
#-----------------------------
""")
void CsvSourceWithTextBlockExample(String nickName, int score) {
    //...
}

[1] ninja, 700
[2] assassin, 850
[3] happy time, 1_100
[4] @user-01x0w, 0xE3F

@CsvFileSource

Gets the CSV file and specifies it as an argument. The useHeadersInDisplayName, delimiter attribute is the same as @CsvSource, and the quoteCharacter attribute is different in that the default quotation marks are double quotes.

#-----------------------------
      NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100
#-----------------------------
  @user-01x0w  |    0xE3F
#-----------------------------

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromClasspathExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(files = "src/test/resources/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromFileExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[{index}] {arguments}")
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", useHeadersInDisplayName = true, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceAndHeadersExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

The output results are omitted because they have already been covered a lot.

Null and Empty Sources

  • @EmptySource : Specifies an empty string as an argument value.
  • @NullSource : Specifies null as the argument value.
  • @NullAndEmptySource : It is an annotation that combines the two above.
import org.junit.platform.commons.util.StringUtils;

@ParameterizedTest
@NullSource
@EmptySource
@ValueSource(strings = { " ", "   ", "\t", "\n" })
void nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String text) {
    assertTrue(StringUtils.isBlank(text));
}

[1] null    nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[1] 0.012s  passed
[2]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[2]    0s      passed
[3]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[3]    0s      passed
[4]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[4]    0s      passed
[5]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[5]    0s      passed
[6]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[6]    0.001s  passed

@EnumSource

Specifies the Enum constant as an argument. For the value attribute, you must enter an enum class. This value is optional.

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(ChronoUnit.class)
void enumSourceExample(TemporalUnit unit) {
    assertNotNull(unit);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource
void enumSourceWithoutValueExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertNotNull(unit);
}

::v-alert ūüí° If you omit the value attribute, you will not receive automatic completion support for the names attribute, so please keep this in mind. Checked in Intelli J 2023.1.2. ::

In the names attribute, enter the name of the enum constant as a string. This value is optional, in which case all constants are applied.

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(value = ChronoUnit.class, names = { "FOREVER", "ERAS" })
void enumSourceIncludeExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertTrue(EnumSet.of(ChronoUnit.FOREVER, ChronoUnit.ERAS).contains(unit));
}

The mode attribute specifies the mode for which constant to select in the enum class. INCLUDE, EXCLUDE, MATCH_ALL and MATCH_ANY can be used.

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(mode = Mode.EXCLUDE, names = { "CENTURIES", "DECADES" })
void EnumSourceExcludeExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertFalse(EnumSet.of(ChronoUnit.CENTURIES, ChronoUnit.DECADES).contains(unit));
}

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(mode = Mode.MATCH_ALL, names = "^.*DAYS$")
void enumSourceRegexExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertTrue(unit.name().endsWith("DAYS"));
}

@MethodSource

Specify arguments through one or more factory methods. The factory method within the test class should be a static method unless the life cycle of the test class is PER_CLASS, and the factory method of the external class should always be a static method.

The factory method should return Stream<T>. If a single argument is required, just return the Stream of the method's argument type.

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource("fruitProvider")
void explicitLocalMethodSourceExample(String parameter) {
    assertNotNull(parameter);
}

static Stream<String> fruitProvider() {
    return Stream.of("cherry", "durian");
}

It also supports stream for primitive types such as DoubleStream, IntStream, and LongStream.

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource("intRange")
void intStreamMethodSourceExample(int parameter) {
    assertNotEquals(9, parameter);
}

static IntStream intRange() {
    return IntStream.rangeClosed(1, 20).skip(10);
}

If multiple arguments are required, return Stream<Arguments> of type arguments(Object...).

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource("workingSchedule")
void multiParamsMethodSourceExample(String name, int workingHours, Set<DayOfWeek> dayOfWeeks) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertTrue(workingHours >= 1 && workingHours <= 10);
    assertEquals(2, dayOfWeeks.size());
}

static Stream<Arguments> workingSchedule() {
    return Stream.of(
            arguments("John", 3, EnumSet.of(DayOfWeek.MONDAY, DayOfWeek.WEDNESDAY)),
            arguments("Sam", 9, EnumSet.of(DayOfWeek.TUESDAY, DayOfWeek.THURSDAY))
    );
}

Refer to the example below when referring to the external factory method.

.
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ src
    ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ main
    ‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ ...
    ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ test
        ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ java
        ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ ...
        ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ xyz
        ‚Ēā   ‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ besfir
        ‚Ēā   ‚Ēā       ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ StringsProviders.java
        ‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ ExternalMethodSourceDemo.java
        ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ resources

import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest;
import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.MethodSource;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertNotNull;

class ExternalMethodSourceDemo {

    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("some.source.StringsProviders#theSkyIsBlue")
    void externalMethodSourceExample(String parameter) {
        assertNotNull(parameter);
    }
    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("some.source.StringsProviders$NestingProviders#theFruits")
    void externalMethodSourceWithNestingClassExample(String parameter) {
        assertNotNull(parameter);
    }
}

package xyz.besfir;

import java.util.stream.Stream;

class StringsProviders {

    static Stream<String> theSkyIsBlue() {
        return Stream.of("The", "sky", "is", "blue");
    }

    class NestingProviders {

        static Stream<String> theFruits() {
            return Stream.of("apple", "banana", "cherry", "durian");
        }
    }
}

If the factory method name and the @ParameterizedTest annotated method name are the same, it can be omitted.

@ParameterizedTest
@MethodSource
void defaultLocalMethodSourceExample(String argument) {
    assertNotNull(argument);
}

static Stream<String> defaultLocalMethodSourceExample() {
    return Stream.of("apple", "banana");
}

@ArgumentsSource

Used to specify a custom ArgumentsProvider. The source annotations summarized above are internally implementing functions using @ArgumentsSource and ArgumentsProvider.

@Target({ ElementType.ANNOTATION_TYPE, ElementType.METHOD })
@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
@Documented
@API(status = STABLE, since = "5.7")
@ArgumentsSource(ValueArgumentsProvider.class)
public @interface ValueSource {
  ...
}

You can use it if you want to implement it additionally in addition to the source annotation provided.

@RepeatedTest

Repeat the test as many times as you specify.

@RepeatedTest(10)
void equalsTest() {
    assertEquals(1 + 1, 2);
}

@Nested

Used to group tests, only for non-static nested classes. Before Java 16, @BeforeAll and @AfterAll cannot be used because static methods cannot be declared on nested classes. However, it can be used if the test instance life cycle is PER_CLASS. After Java 16, this is not the case because static methods can be declared on nested classes.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;

class NestedExampleTest {

    @Nested
    class SomeGroup {

        @Nested
        class InnerTest1_1 {

            @Test
            void test1() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }

        @Nested
        class InnerTest1_2 {

            @Test
            void test2() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }
    }


    @Nested
    class OtherGroup {

        @Nested
        class InnerTestA {

            @Test
            void testA() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }

        @Nested
        class InnerTestB {

            @Test
            void testB() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }
    }
}

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/148d3b7e-a650-4e5c-be9f-03e7d840fa25.webp" alt="JUnit panel"}

@Timeout

An annotation that causes an exception to occur and terminate immediately if the method exceeds the specified duration, and can be annotated to a test, test factory, test template, or life cycle method. It can also be annotated to class level and applied in common to multiple methods, and the nested classes annotated @Nested are also affected. However, if set at the class level, it does not apply to the life cycle method.

In terms of description alone, the use seems to be the same as that of assertTimeoutPreemptively(), but @Timeout uses it to replace it in certain scenarios. Because assertTimeoutPreemptively() runs on a different thread than the test method, so spring transaction does not work as desired. On the other hand, @Timeout is basically used in the same thread as the test method, so it is used as an alternative if mentioned above. Thread behavior can be changed through threadMode properties.

  • INFERRED : Default value, and it follows the setting parameter or the setting file's junit.jupiter.execution.timeout.thread.mode.default property value. If not, operate as SAME_THREAD.
  • SAME_THREAD : Runs on the same thread as the test method.
  • SEPARATE_THREAD : It runs on a different thread than the test method.

unit attribute allows you to select a unit of time, and the basic unit is SECONDS. 

@Test
@Timeout(value = 500, unit = TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
void timeoutAnnotationExample() throws InterruptedException {
    Thread.sleep(10_000);
}

@TempDir

Used to create and cleanup temporary directories. You can annotate to a parameter or field-level Path or File type.

@Test
void tempDirExample(@TempDir Path source, @TempDir Path target) throws IOException {
    Path sourceFile = source.resolve("test.txt");
    Files.write(sourceFile, "a,b,c".getBytes());

    Path targetFile = Files.copy(sourceFile, target.resolve("test.txt"));

    assertNotEquals(sourceFile, targetFile);
    assertEquals(singletonList("a,b,c"), Files.readAllLines(targetFile));
}

cleanup attribute allow you to specify cleanup actions, and there are four modes: DEFAULT, ALWAYS, ON_SUCCESS and NEVER DEFAULT mode follows the setting parameter or the setting file's junit.jupiter.tempdir.cleanup.mode.default property value, and operates as ALWAYS mode if there is no value.

Extension Model

Declarative Extension Registration

You can register one or more extensions using @ExtendWith.

@ExtendWith({ DatabaseExtension.class, WebServerExtension.class})
class ExtensionModelTest { }
// or 
@ExtendWith(DatabaseExtension.class)
@ExtendWith(WebServerExtension.class)
class ExtensionModelTest { }

Programmatic Extension Registration

@RegisterExtension is used when you need to put some value into the extension from the outside. The extended field should not be a private field or null.

If the @RegisterExtension field is static, it is registered later than the extensions registered in @ExtendWith. In this case, both the class-level callback and the method-level callback of the extension are invoked.

public class RegisterExtensionTest {

    @RegisterExtension
    static LoggingExtension staticExtension = new LoggingExtension("static version");

    @Test
    public void someTest() { }
}

Type static version In beforeAll : RegisterExtensionTest
Type static version In beforeEach : someTest()

If the @RegisterExtension field is non-static, it is registered later than the extensions specified in @ExtendWith, and only invokes the method-level callback of the extension. 

public class RegisterExtensionTest {

    @RegisterExtension
    LoggingExtension staticExtension = new LoggingExtension("non-static version");

    @Test
    public void someTest() { }
}

Type non-static version In beforeEach : someTest()

However, if the life cycle of the test instance is PER_CLASS, it is registered before the extensions specified in @ExtendWith, and both class-level and method-level callbacks of the extension are invoked.

@TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS)
public class RegisterExtensionTest {

    @RegisterExtension
    LoggingExtension staticExtension = new LoggingExtension("static version");

    @Test
    public void someTest() { }
}

Type non-static version In beforeAll : RegisterExtensionTest
Type non-static version In beforeEach : someTest()

public class LoggingExtension implements BeforeAllCallback, BeforeEachCallback {

    private final String type;

    public LoggingExtension(String type) {
        this.type = type;
    }

    @Override
    public void beforeAll(ExtensionContext extensionContext) throws Exception {
        System.out.printf("Type %s In beforeAll : %s\n", type, extensionContext.getDisplayName());
    }

    @Override
    public void beforeEach(ExtensionContext extensionContext) throws Exception {
        System.out.printf("Type %s In beforeEach : %s\n", type, extensionContext.getDisplayName());
    }

    public String getType() {
        return type;
    }
}

JUnit Platform configuration file

The file must be in the src/test/resources/junit-platform.properties path and can be configured for JUnit global settings.

junit.jupiter.execution.timeout.thread.mode.default = same_thread
junit.jupiter.tempdir.cleanup.mode.default = always
...

Customizing display name

Repeatable tests, such as @RepeatedTest and @ParameterizedTest, can customize the invocation display name with the name attribute to identify each test. In this case, since the name attribute is a message format pattern, it must be used twice to display one small quotation mark. The supported placeholders are as follows.

| Placeholder | Description | |----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------| | {displayName} | the display name of the method | | {index} | the current invocation index (1-based) | | {arguments} | the complete, comma-separated arguments list | | {argumentsWithNames} | the complete, comma-separated arguments list with parameter names | | {0}, {1}, ‚Ä¶ | an individual argument |

Example

@DisplayName("Display name of container")
@ParameterizedTest(name = "{index} ==> the rank of ''{0}'' is {1}")
@CsvSource({ "apple, 1", "banana, 2", "'lemon, lime', 3" })
void customDisplayNamesExample(String fruit, int rank) { }

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/dca1e3c0-b495-499a-85ec-6a0bddb96ede.webp" alt="JUnit panel with name attribute"}

Reference

  • JUnit user guide
  • JUnit5 Programmatic Extension Registration with @RegisterExtension

@CsvFileSource

CSV ŪĆĆžĚľžĚĄ ÍįÄž†łžôÄžĄú žĚłžąėŽ°ú žßÄž†ēŪēúŽč§. useHeadersInDisplayName, delimiter žÜćžĄĪžĚÄ @CsvSourceÍ≥ľ ÍįôžúľŽ©į, quoteCharacter žÜćžĄĪžĚÄ ÍłįŽ≥ł ŽĒįžėīŪĎúÍįÄ ŪĀį ŽĒįžėīŪĎúžĚł ž†źžĚī Žč§Ž•īŽč§.

#-----------------------------
      NAME     |    SCORE
#-----------------------------
     ninja     |     700
#-----------------------------
    assassin   |     850
#-----------------------------
  "happy time" |    1_100

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromClasspathExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@CsvFileSource(files = "src/test/resources/example.csv", numLinesToSkip = 1, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceFromFileExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

@ParameterizedTest(name = "[{index}] {arguments}")
@CsvFileSource(resources = "/example.csv", useHeadersInDisplayName = true, delimiter = '|')
void CsvFileSourceAndHeadersExample(String name, int score) {
    assertNotNull(name);
    assertNotEquals(0, score);
}

ž∂úŽ†• Í≤įÍ≥ľŽäĒ žĚīŽĮł ŽßéžĚī Žč§Ž§ėžúľŽĮÄŽ°ú žÉĚŽěĶŪēúŽč§.

Null and Empty Sources

  • @EmptySource: žĚłžąė ÍįížúľŽ°ú ŽĻąŽ¨łžěźžóīžĚĄ žßÄž†ēŪēúŽč§.
  • @NullSource: žĚłžąė ÍįížúľŽ°ú nullžĚĄ žßÄž†ēŪēúŽč§.
  • @NullAndEmptySource : žúĄžĚė 2ÍįúŽ•ľ Ūē©žĻú žĖīŽÖłŪÖĆžĚīžÖėžĚīŽč§.
import org.junit.platform.commons.util.StringUtils;

@ParameterizedTest
@NullSource
@EmptySource
@ValueSource(strings = { " ", "   ", "\t", "\n" })
void nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String text) {
    assertTrue(StringUtils.isBlank(text));
}

[1] null    nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[1] 0.012s  passed
[2]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[2]    0s      passed
[3]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[3]    0s      passed
[4]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[4]    0s      passed
[5]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[5]    0s      passed
[6]         nullEmptyAndBlankStrings(String)[6]    0.001s  passed

@EnumSource

Enum žÉĀžąėŽ•ľ žĚłžąėŽ°ú žßÄž†ēŪēúŽč§. value žÜćžĄĪžóźŽäĒ enum ŪĀīŽěėžä§Ž•ľ žěÖŽ†•Ūēīžēľ ŪēúŽč§. žĚī ÍįížĚÄ žÉĚŽěĶ ÍįÄŽä•ŪēėŽč§. 

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource(ChronoUnit.class)
void enumSourceExample(TemporalUnit unit) {
    assertNotNull(unit);
}

@ParameterizedTest
@EnumSource
void enumSourceWithoutValueExample(ChronoUnit unit) {
    assertNotNull(unit);

import org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest;
import org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.MethodSource;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertNotNull;

class ExternalMethodSourceDemo {

    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("some.source.StringsProviders#theSkyIsBlue")
    void externalMethodSourceExample(String parameter) {
        assertNotNull(parameter);
    }
    @ParameterizedTest
    @MethodSource("some.source.StringsProviders$NestingProviders#theFruits")
    void externalMethodSourceWithNestingClassExample(String parameter) {
        assertNotNull(parameter);
    }
}

package xyz.besfir;

import java.util.stream.Stream;

class StringsProviders {

    static Stream<String> theSkyIsBlue() {
        return Stream.of("The", "sky", "is", "blue");
    }

    class NestingProviders {

        static Stream<String> theFruits() {
            return Stream.of("apple", "banana", "cherry", "durian");
        }
    }
}

}

static Stream<String> defaultLocalMethodSourceExample() {
    return Stream.of("apple", "banana");
}

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Nested;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;

class NestedExampleTest {

    @Nested
    class SomeGroup {

        @Nested
        class InnerTest1_1 {

            @Test
            void test1() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }

        @Nested
        class InnerTest1_2 {

            @Test
            void test2() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }
    }


    @Nested
    class OtherGroup {

        @Nested
        class InnerTestA {

            @Test
            void testA() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }

        @Nested
        class InnerTestB {

            @Test
            void testB() {
                assertTrue(true);
            }
        }
    }
}

žěĄžčú ŽĒĒŽ†ČŪĄįŽ¶¨Ž•ľ žÉĚžĄĪŪēėÍ≥† ž†ēŽ¶¨Ūē† ŽēĆ žā¨žö©ŪēúŽč§. ŪĆĆŽĚľŽĮłŪĄįŽāė ŪēĄŽďú žąėž§ÄžĚė PathŽāė FileŪÉÄžěÖžóź žĄ†žĖłŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

@Test
void tempDirExample(@TempDir Path source, @TempDir Path target) throws IOException {
    Path sourceFile = source.resolve("test.txt");
    Files.write(sourceFile, "a,b,c".getBytes());

    Path targetFile = Files.copy(sourceFile, target.resolve("test.txt"));

    assertNotEquals(sourceFile, targetFile);
    assertEquals(singletonList("a,b,c"), Files.readAllLines(targetFile));
}

public class RegisterExtensionTest {

Type non-static version In beforeAll : RegisterExtensionTest
Type non-static version In beforeEach : someTest()

public class LoggingExtension implements BeforeAllCallback, BeforeEachCallback {

    private final String type;

    public LoggingExtension(String type) {
        this.type = type;
    }

    @Override
    public void beforeAll(ExtensionContext extensionContext) throws Exception {
        System.out.printf("Type %s In beforeAll : %s\n", type, extensionContext.getDisplayName());
    }

    @Override
    public void beforeEach(ExtensionContext extensionContext) throws Exception {
        System.out.printf("Type %s In beforeEach : %s\n", type, extensionContext.getDisplayName());
    }

    public String getType() {
        return type;
    }
}

@RepeatedTest, @ParameterizedTestžôÄ ÍįôžĚī ŽįėŽ≥Ķ ÍįÄŽä•Ūēú ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽäĒ ÍįĀ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłžĚė žčĚŽ≥ĄžĚĄ žúĄŪēīžĄú name žÜćžĄĪžúľŽ°ú ŪĎúžčúŽźėŽäĒ žĚīŽ¶ĄžĚĄ žā¨žö©žěź ž†ēžĚėŪē† žąė žěąŽč§. žĚīŽēĆ, name žÜćžĄĪžĚÄ Ž©ĒžĄłžßÄ ŪŹ¨Ž©ß ŪĆ®ŪĄīžĚīŽĮÄŽ°ú ŪēėŽāėžĚė žěĎžĚÄ ŽĒįžėīŪĎúŽ•ľ ŪĎúžčúŪēėÍłį žúĄŪēīžĄ†, ŽĎź Ž≤ą žā¨žö©ŪēīžēľŪēúŽč§. žßÄžõźŽźėŽäĒ placeholderŽäĒ žēĄŽěėžôÄ ÍįôŽč§.

| Placeholder | Description | |----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------| | {displayName} | @DisplayNamežĚī žěąžúľŽ©ī žĚīŽ•ľ ÍįÄž†łžė§Í≥†, žóÜžúľŽ©ī Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďú žĚīŽ¶ĄžĚĄ ŪĎúžčú. | | {index} | 1Ž∂ÄŪĄį žčúžěĎŪēėŽäĒ ŪėĄžě¨ žĚłŽćĪžä§ ÍįížĚĄ ŪĎúžčú. | | {arguments} | ŪėĄžě¨ žĚłžąėŽ•ľ žĹ§ŽßąŽ°ú ÍĶ¨Ž∂ĄŪēīžĄú ŪĎúžčúŪēúŽč§. | | {argumentsWithNames} | ŪėĄžě¨ žĚłžąėŽ•ľ žĚłžąėŽ™ÖÍ≥ľ Ūē®ÍĽė žĹ§ŽßąŽ°ú ÍĶ¨Ž∂ĄŪēīžĄú ŪĎúžčúŪēúŽč§.

item.title: Starting unit test with JUnit 5 Part 1
item.description: I've been aware of the importance of testing code for a long time, but I've been delaying them, saying that I'll learn later because I can finish m...
item.content:encoded:
I've been aware of the importance of testing code for a long time, but I've been delaying them, saying that I'll learn later because I can finish my work faster if I write a code during the time I study for the testing code. However, whenever the requirements were added little by little, I suddenly felt ashamed of myself for testing them in a fist style. Therefore, I am going to write a series to Practice test code for personal study purposes.

In this article, I will summarize the information that must be known, but can proceed with the test properly.

Unit test

A unit test is a procedure in computer programming to verify that certain modules of source code work exactly as intended. That is, it refers to the procedure of writing a test case for all functions and methods.

Korean Wikipedia

Advantages

  • Detect bug: The functionality is tested in small pieces, so you can quickly check where the problem occurred.
  • Refactoring: ensures better accuracy and stability in refectoring because it makes the code small and simple.
  • Documentation: Because there are libraries that use unit tests to document APIs, documentation and maintenance become convenient.

Disadvantages and Limitations

  • Ensuring results: because the method is isolated and simulated, the results of the unit test do not always represent the results of the system as a whole.
  • Development and Maintenance Costs: Test codes also need to be developed and maintained, increasing the scope to be managed.

JUnit 5

Unlike the previous JUnit, JUnit 5 consists of JUnit Platform + JUnit Jupiter + JUnit Vintage.

  • JUnit Platform: It is responsible for executing the test framework on the JVM. It also provides a test engine API to facilitate integration with JUnit.
  • JUnit Jupiter: It provides a test engine for Jupiter subprojects such as JUnit 5.
  • JUnit Vintage: It provides a test engine for running JUnit 3 and JUnit 4 based tests.

Basic annotations

Since it is frequently used, I am going to organize the annotations that I basically need to know.

  • @Test: Indicates that the method is a test method.
  • @BeforeAll: Indicates that this is the first method to be executed within the class.
  • @AfterAll: Indicates that this is the last method to be executed within the class.
  • @BeforeEach: Indicates that this is a method that must be executed before each test method in the class.
  • @AfterEach: Indicates that this is a method that must be executed after each test method in the class.

Lifecycle methods

@BeforeAll, @AfterAll, @BeforeEach, and @AfterEach are called life cycle methods because they run according to the life cycle of the test.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.AfterAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.AfterEach;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Disabled;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

public class OrderTest {

    @BeforeAll
    static void beforeAll() {
        System.out.println("=-=-=-=-=-=");
        System.out.println("Before all");
    }

    @AfterAll
    static void afterAll() {
        System.out.println("After all");
        System.out.println("=-=-=-=-=-=");
    }

    @BeforeEach
    void setUp() {
        System.out.println("-----");
        System.out.println("Before each");
    }

    @AfterEach
    void tearDown() {
        System.out.println("After each");
        System.out.println("-----");
    }

    @Test
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }

    @Test
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

    @Test
    @Disabled
    void disableTest() {
        System.out.println("it does not run");
    }
}

=-=-=-=-=-=
Before all
-----
Before each
run last
After each
-----
-----
Before each
run first
After each
-----
After all
=-=-=-=-=-=

@DisplayName

If you look at IDE's JUnit panel or Gradle test report, you can check the results we tested. In this case, each test can be identified by a method name.

class DisplayNameTest {

    @Test
    void displayNameContainingSpacesAndYourLanguageExample() { }

    @Test
    void displayNameContainingAngryCharactersExample() { }

    @Test
    void displayNameContainingEmojiExample() { }
}

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/b82f88bc-bc86-4b03-8cc7-dd0d8fc65865.webp" alt="JUnit panel"}

However, sometimes you may want to change only the test name without modifying the method name, and the one you use at this time is @DisplayName.

@DisplayName("Custom test class name")
class DisplayNameTest {

    @Test
    @DisplayName("ŪēúÍłÄŽŹĄ žěė ŪĎúžčúŽźúŽč§")
    void displayNameContainingSpacesAndYourLanguageExample() { }

    @Test
    @DisplayName("ŗ≠ß(ŗĻĎ‚ÄĘŐÄŠóĚ‚ÄĘŐĀ)ŗę≠")
    void displayNameContainingAngryCharactersExample() { }

    @Test
    @DisplayName("ūüĆą")
    void displayNameContainingEmojiExample() { }
}

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/acc1d21e-482e-4e6f-8228-3147e1baff9b.webp" alt="JUnit panel with @DisplayName"}

Test Order

-----
Before each
run last
After each
-----
-----
Before each
run first     # Why is this here? ¬Į\_(„ÉĄ)_/¬Į
After each

Let's take a look at the log of the Lifecycle Method unit again. I wrote runFirst() first, and even if I think about it in alphabetical order, it would be run first, but it did later.

This is because JUnit runs the tests in an unpredictable order, as unit tests should not normally depend on order. However, sometimes the order of testing may be important. In this case, annotate your test class or test interface with @TestMethodOrder and specify the desired MethodOrderer implementation.

OrderAnnotation

Sort the methods in ascending order based on the value specified in the @Order annotation. The default order value is Integer.MAX_VALUE / 2, and a method larger than this value runs later than a method without @Order annotation.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.MethodOrderer.OrderAnnotation;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Order;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.TestMethodOrder;

@TestMethodOrder(OrderAnnotation.class)
public class TestMethodOrderTest {

    @Test
    @Order(1)
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }

    @Test
    @Order(2)
    void runSecond() {
        System.out.println("run second");
    }

    @Test
    void runThird() {
        System.out.println("run third");
    }

    @Test
    @Order(Integer.MAX_VALUE / 2 + 1)
    void run4th() {
        System.out.println("run 4th");
    }

    @Test
    @Order(Integer.MAX_VALUE)
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

}

run first
run second
run third
run 4th
run last

MethodName

Sorts test methods alphanumerically based on their names and formal parameter lists.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.MethodOrderer.MethodName;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.TestMethodOrder;

@TestMethodOrder(MethodName.class)
public class TestMethodOrderTest {

    @Test
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

    @Test
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }
}

run first
run last

DisplayName

Sorts test methods alphanumerically based on their display names.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.DisplayName;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.MethodOrderer;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.MethodOrderer.Random;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.TestMethodOrder;

@TestMethodOrder(MethodOrderer.DisplayName.class)
public class TestMethodOrderTest {

    @Test
    @DisplayName("run last")
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

    @Test
    @DisplayName("run first")
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }
}

run first
run last

Random

It is randomly sorted using System.nanoTime() as a seed. It does not always guarantee the same order of execution.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.MethodOrderer.Random;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.TestMethodOrder;

@TestMethodOrder(Random.class)
public class TestMethodOrderTest {

    @Test
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }

    @Test
    void runSecond() {
        System.out.println("run second");
    }

    @Test
    void runThird() {
        System.out.println("run third");
    }

    @Test
    void run4th() {
        System.out.println("run 4th");
    }

    @Test
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

}

run last
run 4th
run third
run first
run second

Assertions

The task of verifying the results of a test is called assertion, and Assertions, the title of this section, refers to a collection of asserting utility methods. So, all the asserting utility methods are in the org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions. From now on, the method in this class will be called the assertion method.

Assertion methods optionally receive an error message in the last parameter. It can be handed over to a lambda that returns a string type or string, and when using lambda, it is called only when the test fails, so it seems that it can help optimize performance a little.

assertEquals() & assertNotEquals()

Used to verify that the values are the same / different.

@Test
void equalsTest() {
    assertEquals(1 + 1, 2);
}


@Test
void notEqualsTest() {
    assertNotEquals(1 + 1, 3);
}

assertNull() & assertNotNull()

Used to verify that the values is null / not null.

@Test
void nullTest() {
    assertNull(null);
}

@Test
void notNullTest() {
    assertNotNull(1);
}

assertTrue() & assertFalse()

Used to verify that the values is true / false.

@Test
void trueTest() {
    assertTrue(1 < 2);
}

@Test
void falseTest() {
    assertFalse(1 > 2);
}

assertSame() & assertNotSame()

Used to verify that the values are the same object / different object.

@Test
void sameTest() {
    String expected = "expected";
    assertSame(expected, "expected");
}

@Test
void notSameTest() {
    String expected = "expected";
    assertNotSame(expected, new String("expected"));
}

assertArrayEquals()

Used to verify that the values of the two arrays match.

@Test
void arrayEqualsTest() {
    char[] expected = {'e', 'x', 'p', 'e', 'c', 't', 'e', 'd'};
    char[] actual = "expected".toCharArray();
    assertArrayEquals(expected, actual);
}

assertIterableEquals()

It is used to verify that the values of the two Iterable implementations match. The values must be returned in the same order and do not necessarily have to be of the same type.

@Test
void iterableEqualsTest() {
    Iterable<String> expected = new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList("Hello", "Junit", "Practice"));
    Iterable<String> actual = new LinkedHashSet<>(Arrays.asList("Hello", "Junit", "Practice"));

    assertIterableEquals(expected, actual);
}

assertLinesMatch()

It is used to verify that the strings of the two lists match, and each element performs the following steps from the beginning.

  1. Check that the values of each element are the same. - If the same, move on to the next element.
  2. If not, check the regular expression with String.matches(). - If the same, move on to the next element.
  3. If not, check if it is a fast-forward marker. - If the same, move on to the next element.

```java[Case for step 2] @Test void linesMatchTestCaseForStep2() { List expected = Arrays.asList("Today's ", ".*", "\d+"); List actual = Arrays.asList("Today's ", "lucky number is ", "72"); assertLinesMatch(expected, actual); }

```java[Case for step 3]
@Test
void linesMatchTestCaseForStep3() {
    List<String> expected = Arrays.asList(">>2>>", "blue");
    List<String> actual = Arrays.asList("The sky ", "is ", "blue");
    assertLinesMatch(expected, actual);
}

assertThrows()

Used to verify that the expected exception type is thrown.

@Test
void throwsTest() {

    Exception exception = assertThrows(Exception.class, () -> System.out.println(1/0));
    assertEquals("/ by zero", exception.getMessage());
}

assertThrowsExactly()

Used to verify that the expected exception is thrown.

@Test
void ThrowsExactlyTest() {

    Exception exception = assertThrowsExactly(ArithmeticException.class, () -> System.out.println(1/0));
    assertEquals("/ by zero", exception.getMessage());
}

assertTimeout()

Used to verify completion before the expected time value is exceeded. Even if the time is exceeded, the test runs until the end, and an error message shows how long it has passed.

import static java.time.Duration.ofMillis;

@Test
void timeoutTest() {
    String actualResult = assertTimeout(ofMillis(10), () -> "Hello world!");
    assertEquals("Hello world!", actualResult);
}

import static java.time.Duration.ofMillis;
@Test
void timeoutTestExceeded() {
    assertTimeout(ofMillis(10), () -> {
        Thread.sleep(100);
    });
}

```[Failed output] execution exceeded timeout of 10 ms by 92 ms

### `assertTimeoutPreemptively()`

Used to verify completion before the expected **time value is exceeded**.
The test **fails immediately** when the time is exceeded.

java @Test void TimeoutPreemptivelyTest() { assertTimeoutPreemptively(ofMillis(10), () -> { Thread.sleep(100); }); }

```[Output]
execution timed out after 10 ms

assertAll()

The assertion method does not execute subsequent code because an exception occurs when the test fails. However, there may be cases where you want to run all assertion methods regardless of failure. In that case, if you use this method to group the assertion methods, they do not cause an exception, so even if they fail, they do not stop immediately and aggregate the errors.

But, if an exception (e.g., Out of Memory) is generated in the blacklist, the execution is immediately stopped and overwritten with an unchecked exception.

@Test
void assertAllTest() {
    assertAll(
            "Group name",
            () -> assertTrue(1 < 2),
            () -> fail("Occur manually"),
            () -> assertTrue(1 > 2)
    );
}

Group name (2 failures)
    org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: Occur manually
    org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: expected: <true> but was: <false>
Expected :true
Actual   :false

fail()

Force the test to fail.

@Test
void assertEachTest() {
    fail("Occur manually");
}

Occur manually
org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: Occur manually
    at app//org.junit.jupiter.api.AssertionUtils.fail(AssertionUtils.java:38)
    ...

@TestInstance

JUnit creates a new test instance for each test method to minimize the impact between tests. However, you may not want to create a new test instance every time as needed.

In this case, you can annotate the class @TestInstance to change the test instance creation pattern according to the mode you specify. The selectable modes are PER_METHOD and PER_CLASS.

  • PER_METHOD: It is the default value and is characterized by not sharing the state because it creates a new instance for each method.

  • @BeforeAll and @AfterAll must be declared in the static method.

  • PER_CLASS: It is possible to share the status in the method because each class creates a new instance.

  • @BeforeAll and @AfterAll do not necessarily have to be declared in the static method.

Why do we need to understand the life cycle of a test instance

If we don't know the life cycle of the test instance, we write the code on the premise that we can share the state because we think in PER_CLASS mode. Therefore, the following cases may occur.

@TestMethodOrder(MethodOrderer.OrderAnnotation.class)
class LifeCycleTest {

    int counter = 0;

    @Test
    @Order(1)
    void counterShouldBe1() {
        System.out.printf("Expected: %d, Actual: %d\n", 1, ++counter);
    }
    @Test
    @Order(2)
    void counterShouldBe2() {
        System.out.printf("Expected: %d, Actual: %d\n", 2, ++counter);
    }
}

Expected: 1, Actual: 1
Expected: 2, Actual: 1

We expected to increase by 1 for each test, but because a new instance was created for each test method, the counter would start from zero every time.

Reference

  • JUnit user guide
  • Assertions in JUnit 4 and JUnit 5
  • Wikipedia

item.description: ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĹĒŽďúžĚė ž§ĎžöĒžĄĪžĚÄ žĚīž†ĄŽ∂ÄŪĄį žĚłžßÄŪēėÍ≥† žěąžóąžßÄŽßĆ, Í∑łÍĪł žĚĶŪěź žčúÍįĄžóź žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ žßúŽ©ī ŽćĒ ŽĻ†Ž•īÍ≤Ć ŽßąŽ¨īŽ¶¨ Ūē† žąė žěąžúľŽčą Žāėž§Ďžóź Í≥ĶŽ∂ÄŪēīžēľÍ≤†Žč§Ž©į Í≥ĄžÜć žĚīŽ§ĄžôĒŽč§. ŪēėžßÄŽßĆ, žöĒÍĶ¨žā¨Ūē≠žĚī ž°įÍłąžĒ© ž∂ĒÍįÄŽź† ŽēĆŽßąŽč§ ž£ľŽ®ĻÍĶ¨ÍĶ¨žčĚžúľŽ°ú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŪēėÍ≥† žěąŽäĒ žä§žä§Ž°úžóźÍ≤Ć Ž¨łŽďĚ Ž∂ÄŽĀĄŽü¨žõÄžĚĄ ŽäźÍľąŽč§. ŽĒį...
item.content:encoded:
ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĹĒŽďúžĚė ž§ĎžöĒžĄĪžĚÄ žĚīž†ĄŽ∂ÄŪĄį žĚłžßÄŪēėÍ≥† žěąžóąžßÄŽßĆ, Í∑łÍĪł žĚĶŪěź žčúÍįĄžóź žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ žßúŽ©ī ŽćĒ ŽĻ†Ž•īÍ≤Ć ŽßąŽ¨īŽ¶¨ Ūē† žąė žěąžúľŽčą Žāėž§Ďžóź Í≥ĶŽ∂ÄŪēīžēľÍ≤†Žč§Ž©į Í≥ĄžÜć ŽĮłŽ§ĄžôĒŽč§. ŪēėžßÄŽßĆ, žöĒÍĶ¨žā¨Ūē≠žĚī ž°įÍłąžĒ© ž∂ĒÍįÄŽź† ŽēĆŽßąŽč§ ž£ľŽ®ĻÍĶ¨ÍĶ¨žčĚžúľŽ°ú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŪēėÍ≥† žěąŽäĒ žä§žä§Ž°úžóźÍ≤Ć Ž¨łŽďĚ Ž∂ÄŽĀĄŽü¨žõÄžĚĄ ŽäźÍľąŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú ÍįúžĚł Í≥ĶŽ∂Ä Ž™©ž†ĀžúľŽ°ú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĹĒŽďúžóź žĚĶžąôŪēīžßÄÍłį žčúŽ¶¨ž¶ąŽ•ľ žć®Ž≥īŽ†§ ŪēúŽč§.

žĚīŽ≤ą ÍłÄžóźžĄúŽäĒ ŽįėŽďúžčú žēĆžēĄžēľŽßĆ, ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽ•ľ žė¨ŽįĒŽ•īÍ≤Ć žßĄŪĖČŪē† žąė žěąŽäĒ ž†ēŽ≥īŽď§žĚĄ ž†ēŽ¶¨Ūēī Ž≥ľ žėąž†ēžĚīŽč§.

Žč®žúĄ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł

Žč®žúĄ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽäĒ žĽīŪď®ŪĄį ŪĒĄŽ°úÍ∑łŽěėŽįćžóźžĄú žÜĆžä§ žĹĒŽďúžĚė ŪäĻž†ē Ž™®ŽďąžĚī žĚėŽŹĄŽźú ŽĆÄŽ°ú ž†ēŪôēŪěą žěĎŽŹôŪēėŽäĒžßÄ Í≤Äž¶ĚŪēėŽäĒ ž†ąžį®Žč§. ž¶Č, Ž™®Žď† Ūē®žąėžôÄ Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďúžóź ŽĆÄŪēú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žľÄžĚīžä§Ž•ľ žěĎžĄĪŪēėŽäĒ ž†ąžį®Ž•ľ ŽßźŪēúŽč§.

žúĄŪā§ŪĒľŽĒĒžēĄ Žįúž∑Ć

Žč®žúĄ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłžĚė žě•ž†ź

  • Ž¨łž†úž†ź ŪĆĆžēÖ: ÍłįŽä•žĚĄ žěĎÍ≤Ć ž™ľÍįúžĄú ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź žĖīŽĖ§ Ž∂ÄŽ∂ĄžóźžĄú Ž¨łž†úÍįÄ žÉĚÍ≤ľŽäĒžßÄ ŽĻ†Ž•īÍ≤Ć ŪĆĆžēÖŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.
  • Ž¶¨ŪĆ©ŪÜ†ŽßĀ: žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ žěĎÍ≥† Žč®žąúŪēėÍ≤Ć ŽßĆŽď§Íłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź Ž¶¨ŪéôŪÜ†ŽßĀ žčúžóź ŽćĒ ŽāėžĚÄ ž†ēŪôēžĄĪÍ≥ľ žēąž†ēžĄĪžĚĄ Ž≥īžě•ŪēúŽč§.

@BeforeAll, @AfterAll, @BeforeEach, @AfterEachŽäĒ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłžĚė žÉĚŽ™Öž£ľÍłįžóź ŽĒįŽĚľ žč§ŪĖČŽźėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź žÉĚŽ™Öž£ľÍłį Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďúŽĚľÍ≥† Ž∂ÄŽ•łŽč§.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.AfterAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.AfterEach;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Disabled;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

public class OrderTest {

    @BeforeAll
    static void beforeAll() {
        System.out.println("=-=-=-=-=-=");
        System.out.println("Before all");
    }

    @AfterAll
    static void afterAll() {
        System.out.println("After all");
        System.out.println("=-=-=-=-=-=");
    }

    @BeforeEach
    void setUp() {
        System.out.println("-----");
        System.out.println("Before each");
    }

    @AfterEach
    void tearDown() {
        System.out.println("After each");
        System.out.println("-----");
    }

    @Test
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }

    @Test
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

    @Test
    @Disabled
    void disableTest() {
        System.out.println("it does not run");
    }
}

OrderAnnotation

@Orderžóź žßÄž†ēŪēú ÍįížĚĄ Íłįž§ÄžúľŽ°ú Ž©ĒžĄúŽďúŽ•ľ žė§Ž¶Ąžį®žąúžúľŽ°ú ž†ēŽ†¨Ūē©ŽčąŽč§. ÍłįŽ≥ł žąúžĄú ÍįížĚÄ Integer.MAX_VALUE / 2žĚīŽ©į, žĚī ÍįíŽ≥īŽč§ ŪĀį Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďúŽäĒ žĖīŽÖłŪÖĆžĚīžÖėžĚī žóÜŽäĒ Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďú Ž≥īŽč§ Žāėž§Ďžóź žč§ŪĖČŽźúŽč§.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.MethodOrderer.OrderAnnotation;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Order;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.TestMethodOrder;

@TestMethodOrder(OrderAnnotation.class)
public class TestMethodOrderTest {

    @Test
    @Order(1)
    void runFirst() {
        System.out.println("run first");
    }

    @Test
    @Order(2)
    void runSecond() {
        System.out.println("run second");
    }

    @Test
    void runThird() {
        System.out.println("run third");
    }

    @Test
    @Order(Integer.MAX_VALUE / 2 + 1)
    void run4th() {
        System.out.println("run 4th");
    }

    @Test
    @Order(Integer.MAX_VALUE)
    void runLast() {
        System.out.println("run last");
    }

}

run third

run last

žĚī Í≤Ĺžöį ŪĀīŽěėžä§žóź @TestInstancežĚĄ žĄ†žĖłŪēīžĄú žßÄž†ēŪēú Ž™®Žďúžóź ŽĒįŽĚľ ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĚłžä§ŪĄīžä§ žÉĚžĄĪ ŪĆ®ŪĄīžĚĄ Ž≥ÄÍ≤ĹŪē† žąė žěąŽč§. Ž™®ŽďúŽäĒ PER_METHOD ŽėźŽäĒ PER_CLASSŽ•ľ žĄ†ŪÉĚŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł žĚłžä§ŪĄīžä§žĚė žÉĚŽ™Ö ž£ľÍłįŽ•ľ žĚīŪēīŪēīžēľ ŪēėŽäĒ žĚīžú†

@TestMethodOrder(MethodOrderer.OrderAnnotation.class)
class LifeCycleTest {

    int counter = 0;

    @Test
    @Order(1)
    void counterShouldBe1() {
        System.out.printf("Expected: %d, Actual: %d\n", 1, ++counter);
    }
    @Test
    @Order(2)
    void counterShouldBe2() {
        System.out.printf("Expected: %d, Actual: %d\n", 2, ++counter);
    }
}

Expected: 1, Actual: 1
Expected: 2, Actual: 1

Žß§ ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŽßąŽč§ 1žĒ© ž¶ĚÍįÄŪē† Í≤ÉžĚĄ ÍłįŽĆÄŪĖąžßÄŽßĆ, ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł Ž©ĒžÜĆŽďúŽßąŽč§ žÉąŽ°úžöī žĚłžä§ŪĄīžä§ÍįÄ žÉĚžĄĪŽźźÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź counterŽäĒ Žß§Ž≤ą 0Ž∂ÄŪĄį žčúžěĎŪēėÍ≤Ć ŽźúŽč§.

item.title: Extracting a list of time zones with Java +Kotlin
item.description: I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted. Therefore...
item.content:encoded:
I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted. Therefore, processing was necessary, but I wanted to process it into a code so that it could be easily managed in the future. I remember seeing the API that brings up the list of zones on the time package side, so when I looked it up, I found getAvailableZoneIds(), and I succeeded in extracting the list using it, so I summarized it.

Code

::v-alert Tested with Java 19 ::

import java.time.LocalDateTime;
import java.time.ZoneId;
import java.time.ZoneOffset;
import java.util.Comparator;

class Scratch {
  public static void main(String[] args) {

    LocalDateTime now = LocalDateTime.now();

    ZoneId.getAvailableZoneIds().stream()
            .map(zoneId -> {
              ZoneId zone = ZoneId.of(zoneId);
              return new ZoneIdWithOffset(zoneId, zone.getRules().getOffset(now));
            })
            .sorted(Comparator.comparing(o -> o.offset().getTotalSeconds()))
            .forEach(System.out::println);

  }
}

record ZoneIdWithOffset(String zoneId, ZoneOffset offset) {

  @Override
  public String toString() {
    return String.format("%s (UTC%s)", zoneId, offset.toString().replace("Z", "+00:00"));
  }
}

Etc/GMT+12 (UTC-12:00)
Pacific/Pago_Pago (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Niue (UTC-11:00)
US/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
...

Explanation

record ZoneIdWithOffset(String zoneId, ZoneOffset offset) {

  @Override
  public String toString() {
    return String.format("%s (UTC%s)", zoneId, offset.toString().replace("Z", "+00:00"));
  }
}

The reason for using replace for offset is that if it is +-0, it is written as Z instead of +-xx:xx like other offsets. Therefore, it was used to unify the form.

public static void main(String[] args) {

  LocalDateTime now = LocalDateTime.now();

  ZoneId.getAvailableZoneIds().stream()
          .map(zoneId -> {
            ZoneId zone = ZoneId.of(zoneId);
            return new ZoneIdWithOffset(zoneId, zone.getRules().getOffset(now));
          })
          .sorted(Comparator.comparing(o -> o.offset().getTotalSeconds()))
          .forEach(System.out::println);

}

Without sorting, it is randomly mixed, not in name order or offset order. Therefore, I ordered it in ascending order based on offset.

Kotlin code

It's not that different, but I've also written Kotlin code.

import java.time.LocalDateTime
import java.time.ZoneId
import java.time.ZoneOffset

data class ZoneIdWithOffset(val zoneId: String, val offset: ZoneOffset) {
    override fun toString(): String {
        return String.format("%s (UTC%s)", zoneId, offset.toString().replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    }
}

val now = LocalDateTime.now()
ZoneId.getAvailableZoneIds()
    .map { zoneId ->
        val zone = ZoneId.of(zoneId)
        ZoneIdWithOffset(zoneId, zone.rules.getOffset(now))
    }
    .sortedBy { (_, offset) -> offset.totalSeconds }
    .forEach { v: ZoneIdWithOffset -> println(v) }

item.content:encoded:
ŽčīŽčĻŪēėÍ≥† žěąŽćė žĄúŽĻĄžä§žóźžĄú ŪÉÄžěĄž°īÍ≥ľ žė§ŪĒĄžÖč ž†ēŽ≥īÍįÄ ŪēĄžöĒŪēī Í≤ÄžÉČŪēīŽī§žúľŽāė žõźŪēėŽäĒ ŪėēžčĚžĚĄ žįĺžĚĄ žąė žóÜžóąŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú ÍįÄÍ≥ĶŪēėŽäĒ žěĎžóÖžĚī ŪēĄžöĒŪĖąŽäĒŽćį, ÍłįžôēžĚīŽ©ī ž∂ĒŪõĄ ÍīÄŽ¶¨ŪēėÍłį žČĹŽŹĄŽ°Ě žĹĒŽďúŽ°ú ÍįÄÍ≥ĶŪēėÍ≥† žč∂žóąŽč§. time ŪĆ®Ūā§žßÄ ž™ĹžóźžĄú ž°ī Ž™©Ž°ĚžĚĄ Ž∂ąŽü¨žė§ŽäĒ APIŽ•ľ Žī§Žćė ÍłįžĖĶžĚī ŽāėžĄú žįĺžēĄŽ≥īŽčą, getAvailableZoneIds()Ž•ľ žįĺžĚĄ žąė žěąžóąÍ≥† žĚīŽ•ľ žĚīžö©ŪēīžĄú Ž™©Ž°ĚžĚĄ ž∂Ēž∂úŪēėŽäĒŽćį žĄĪÍ≥ĶŪĖąÍłįžóź ž†ēŽ¶¨Ž•ľ ŪēīŽ≥łŽč§.

žôĄžĄĪ žĹĒŽďú

::v-alert Java 19žóźžĄú ŪÖĆžä§ŪäłŪē®. ::

import java.time.LocalDateTime;
import java.time.ZoneId;
import java.time.ZoneOffset;
import java.util.Comparator;

class Scratch {
  public static void main(String[] args) {

    LocalDateTime now = LocalDateTime.now();

    ZoneId.getAvailableZoneIds().stream()
            .map(zoneId -> {
              ZoneId zone = ZoneId.of(zoneId);
              return new ZoneIdWithOffset(zoneId, zone.getRules().getOffset(now));
            })
            .sorted(Comparator.comparing(o -> o.offset().getTotalSeconds()))
            .forEach(System.out::println);

  }
}

record ZoneIdWithOffset(String zoneId, ZoneOffset offset) {

  @Override
  public String toString() {
    return String.format("%s (UTC%s)", zoneId, offset.toString().replace("Z", "+00:00"));
  }
}

Etc/GMT+12 (UTC-12:00)
Pacific/Pago_Pago (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Niue (UTC-11:00)
US/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
...

ŪēīžĄ§

record ZoneIdWithOffset(String zoneId, ZoneOffset offset) {

  @Override
  public String toString() {
    return String.format("%s (UTC%s)", zoneId, offset.toString().replace("Z", "+00:00"));
  }
}

offsetžóź replaceŽ•ľ žā¨žö©Ūēú žĚīžú†ŽäĒ, +-0žĚľ Í≤Ĺžöį Žč§Ž•ł žė§ŪĒĄžÖčž≤ėŽüľ +-xx:xxŪėēžčĚžĚī žēĄŽčĆ ZŽ°ú ŪĎúÍłįŽźúŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú ŪėēžčĚžĚĄ ŪÜĶžĚľŪēėÍłį žúĄŪēī žā¨žö©ŪĖąŽč§.

public static void main(String[] args) {

  LocalDateTime now = LocalDateTime.now();

  ZoneId.getAvailableZoneIds().stream()
          .map(zoneId -> {
            ZoneId zone = ZoneId.of(zoneId);
            return new ZoneIdWithOffset(zoneId, zone.getRules().getOffset(now));
          })
          .sorted(Comparator.comparing(o -> o.offset().getTotalSeconds()))
          .forEach(System.out::println);

}

ž†ēŽ†¨žĚĄ ŪēėžßÄžēäžúľŽ©ī žĚīŽ¶ĄžąúŽŹĄ žė§ŪĒĄžÖčžąúŽŹĄ žēĄŽčĆ, Ž¨īžěĎžúĄŽ°ú žĄěžó¨žěąŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú ŪēĄžěźŽäĒ žė§ŪĒĄžÖčžĚĄ Íłįž§ÄžúľŽ°ú žė§Ž¶Ąžį®žąú ž†ēŽ†¨ŪĖąŽč§.

žĹĒŪčÄŽ¶į žĹĒŽďú

Í∑łŽč§žßÄ ŪĀ¨Í≤Ć Žč§Ž•īžßĄ žēäžßÄŽßĆ žĹĒŪčÄŽ¶į žĹĒŽďúŽŹĄ žěĎžĄĪŪēīŽī§Žč§.

    override fun toString(): String {

item.title: I bought my first perfume in my life.
item.description: I fell in love with the scent of hand cream that my sister gave me by chance, and I wanted this scent to come from me, not only my hands. I decided...
item.content:encoded:
I fell in love with the scent of hand cream that my sister gave me by chance, and I wanted this scent to come from me, not only my hands. So, when I searched, I sold perfume, and I applied right away because I could get free blotter of six signature products for just the delivery fee.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/109ff08a-1cba-48c7-9f2b-bad511e3b8a9.webp" alt="Nonfiction santal cream handcream"}

I decided in my mind that I should buy only one topknot cream until I receive the blotter. However, when I smelled it, I changed my mind to buy two types because I thought it would feel stuffy in the middle of summer, maybe because of the creamy scent.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/KakaoTalkPhoto2023-06-12-21-30-38.webp" alt="Nonfiction blotter received"}

I don't know much about perfume, so I'm going to organize it lightly and decide to buy it based on this.

Santal Cream

When I smelled it as a blotter, I felt it was a creamy and sweet woody scent and suede scent, so I felt it was a warm scent that felt heavy overall. However, when I sprayed it on the flesh after purchasing it, it was a little different from when I smelled it as a blotter. At first, it smelled clean and sweet, and as it went back, it smelled creamy, adding to the weight. I heard that there is also a fig scent, but I can't express it because I don't know the fig scent.

Due to its name, it seems to be compared a lot to Le Labo Santal 33, but it is similar only at the beginning, and this section is said to pass quickly. Rather, there is an opinion that it is similar to Diptyque Philosicos, so if you have time, it would be better to try it.

| Main note | Seasons | Longevity / Sillage | Price(USD) | |:-------------------:|:--------------------:|:-------------------:|:---------------------------------:| | Vetiver, Sandalwood, Fig, Cardamom | Spring, Fall, Winter | Middle | 1.0oz / 69.00
3.3oz / 155.00 |

Gaiac Flower

I bought it and I'm using it, and I think I bought it really well. I bought it because I didn't have a choice because I excluded other perfumes from the purchase list, but honestly, when I smelled with blotter, I didn't find it very attractive because it was a bit pungent and heavy. I used it because I had to go out on a slightly hot day, but when I sprayed it on the flesh, it was very satisfying because it was cool and light and had an unexpected flower scent. It seems that you can feel the scent of roasted sweet potatoes as an aside story.

It is recommended for those who prefer cool flower scents such as Byredo Rose of No Man's Land or Zara Rose Petal Drops.

| Main note | Seasons | Longevity/Sillage | Price(USD) | |:----------------------:|:------------:|:-----------------:|:----------------------------------:| | Wild Rose, Guaiac, Vanilla, Amber | Spring, Fall | Lower-middle | 1.0oz / 69.00
3.3oz / 155.00 |

Gentle Night

"Oh? It's a very familiar scent." It was the first thought I felt after smelling it. I was glad that it smelled like vanilla and soft and sweet, similar to the skin I used when I was young, but I excluded it from the purchase list because it was not good enough to use as a perfume in my thought.

If you prefer a clean scent such as Le Labo Vanille 44, it will suit your taste, and it is recommended as a common perfume for men and women in their 20s.

| Main note | Seasons | Longevity/Sillage | Price(USD) | |:-------------------------------------------------------------------:|:-------:|:-----------------:|:--------------------------------:| | Suede, White Tea, Fig,Cedarwood | Spring, Fall, Winter | Lower-middle | 1.0oz/ 69.00
3.3oz / 155.00 |

Forget Me Not

Overall, the scent of basil was noticeable and clean and light, but it felt like a slightly wet grassy scent that felt like grass on a cloudy day. I didn't like spicy scents, so I excluded them from the purchase list, but after re-smelling them while writing this, I strangely want to smell them again.

What should I say, it's not a good scent because it's definitely not my taste, but it's so unique that I want to smell it again.

| Main note | Seasons | Longevity/Sillage | Price(USD) | |:-----------------:|:--------------------:|:-----------------:|:----------------------------------:| | Basil, Pink Pepper, Green, Amber | Spring, Summer, Fall | Lower-middle | 1.0oz / 69.00
3.3oz / 155.00 |

For Rest

Personally, there are a lot of unfamiliar scents, so I can't express what scent it is. It's cool and a little pungent, so it smells like a pain relief patch. I don't know if it's because of its name, but it also smells like grass forest at the end of the scent.

| Main note | Seasons | Longevity/Sillage | Price(USD) | |:----------------------:|:-----------:|:-----------------:|:----------------------------------:| | Hinoki, Frankincense, Turkish Rose, Yuzu | All seasons | Middle | 1.0oz / 69.00
3.3oz / 155.00 |

In The Shower

This scent is also unfamiliar, so I can't express it well. It seems similar to Forest, but it feels different, but it's smoky and cool, but it feels a little less pungent.

Personally, it smelled like smoked ham because it smelled a little salty with a smoky smell. I think it feels especially so when I put the blotter off my hand and smell it.

| Main note | Seasons | Longevity/Sillage | Price(USD) | |:-----------------------:|:------------:|:-----------------:|:----------------------------------:| | Patchouli, Bitter Orange, Campari, Guaiac | Fall, Winter | Middle | 1.0oz / 69.00
3.3oz / 155.00 |

I organized my thoughts like above and decided to buy Sangtal Cream and Gaiac Flower. When I searched, there was a non-fiction store in the duty-free shop, so my younger sister bought it for me on her way back from traveling abroad.

I got my first perfume of my life.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/16282a80-1d76-4289-a232-041b0d8169b1.webp" alt="Santal Cream & Gaiac Flower given as a gift"}

Ž•īŽĚľŽ≥ī ŽįĒŽčź44 ÍįôžĚÄ ÍĻĒŽĀĒŪēú ŪĖ•žĚĄ žĄ†ŪėłŪēúŽč§Ž©ī ž∑®ŪĖ•žóź ŽßěžĚĄ Í≤ÉžĚīŽĚľÍ≥† ŪēėŽ©į, 20ŽĆÄ Žā®ŽÖÄÍ≥Ķžö© ŪĖ•žąėŽ°ú ž∂Ēž≤úŪēúŽč§Í≥† ŪēúŽč§.

ŪŹ¨Í≤ü ŽĮł Žāę

ž†Ąž≤īž†ĀžúľŽ°ú ŽįĒžßą ŪĖ•žĚī ŽŹĄŽďúŽĚľžßÄÍ≤Ć ŽäźÍĽīžßÄÍ≥† ÍĻ®ŽĀóŪēėÍ≥† ÍįÄŽ≤ľžöī ŪĖ•ÍłįžėÄŽäĒŽćį, ŪĚźŽ¶įŽā† ŪíÄŽį≠ ŽäźÍĽīžßÄŽäĒ žāīžßĚ ž†ĖžĚÄ ŪíÄŪĖ•ž≤ėŽüľ ŽäźÍĽīžßĄŽč§. 100ml / 158,000 |

ŪŹ¨ Ž†ąžä§Ūäł

ÍįúžĚłž†ĀžúľŽ°ú žÉĚžÜĆŪēú ŪĖ•žĚī ŽßéžĚī ŽāėžĄú Ž¨īžä® ŪĖ•žĚłžßÄ žěė ŪĎúŪėĄžĚĄ Ž™ĽŪēėÍ≤†Žč§. Ž≠ĒÍįÄ žčúžõźŪēėŽ©īžĄúŽŹĄ žēĹÍįĄžĚÄ žēĆžčłŪēīžĄú ŽßąžĻė ŪĆĆžä§ ŽÉĄžÉą ÍįôžĚÄ ŪĖ•ÍłįÍįÄ ŽāėŽäĒ Í≤É ÍįôŽč§. žĚīŽ¶Ą ŽēĆŽ¨łžĚłžßÄ Ž™®Ž•īÍ≤†ŽäĒŽćį ŪĖ•žĚė ŽĀĚžóź ŪíÄ žą≤ ŪĖ•žĚī ŽāėŽäĒÍ≤É ÍįôÍłįŽŹĄ ŪēėŽč§.

| ž£ľžöĒŽÖłŪäł | Í≥Ąž†ąÍįź | žßÄžÜć/ŽįúŪĖ•Ž†• | ÍįÄÍ≤©(žõź) | |:----------------------:|:---:|:------:|:----------------------------------:| | ŪěąŽÖłŽĀľ, ŪĒĄŽěĎŪā®žĄľžä§, ŪĄįŪā§žČ¨ Ž°úž¶ą, žú†žěź | 4Í≥Ąž†ą | ž§Ď | 30ml / 69,000
100ml / 158,000 |

žĚł ŽćĒ žÉ§žõĆ

item.title: Let's find out about requestAnimationFrame()
item.description: In order to implement animation that is difficult to express with CSS alone, JavaScript must be used, and using it without optimization can cause p...
item.content:encoded:
Before we start, we need to use some terms repeatedly, but it's long, so let's define it as a contraction.

  • setTimeout() and setInterval() => timer function
  • requestAnimationFrame() => rAF()

In order to implement animation that is difficult to express with CSS alone, JavaScript must be used, and using it without optimization can cause performance problems. Prior to the release of rAF() api, the timer function was used as an optimization technique, and since then, rAF() has been used.

In this article, we will check what problems the timer function has and compare it with rAF().

Frame loss

Since the timer function operates without considering the frame, it cannot guarantee when the callback function is called. Therefore, if it runs over two frames as shown in the figure below, the animation will not be smooth because it misses one frame.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/54dfd618-50c9-463e-aa62-dd1919651fd6.webp" alt="Frame loss graph. source - https://web.dev/optimize-javascript-execution"}

::v-alert ūüí° How does 16ms in the figure come from?
If you didn't specifically choose refresh rate and buy it, you would be using a monitor at 60Hz refresh rate, which means that the screen is refreshing itself 60 times every second. Therefore, the screen is updated at intervals of 1,000 ms/60 times ‚Čą 16 ms. ::

On the other hand, rAF() is synchronized with the screen scan rate and is executed when the browser is ready to draw the screen. To be more specific, the callback function written in rAF() is registered in the Animation Frame queue, and all callback functions in this queue are processed at once just before the next repaint. Accordingly, it is possible to prevent a problem in which a frame is lost because the callback function is started in the middle.

:v-iframe{ src="https://stackblitz.com/edit/web-platform-jujfzt?ctl=1&embed=1&file=example1.html" style="height: 560px" }

Using the timer function and rAF(), we made an animation in which each box moves 5 px to the right, a total of 1,000 px, as an example code. As mentioned earlier, the timer function is not guaranteed when the callback function will be executed, so frame loss may or may not occur enough to be visually confirmed. In this case, it can be confirmed by repeating it several times.

Waste of system resources

The timer function also works within an inactive window or hidden iframe, wasting system resources, resulting in battery life and performance degradation problems. However, using rAF() saves system resources by stopping work when the window becomes inactive.

To see this for yourself, let's switch the browser to another tab as soon as we run the animation of the example code mentioned above. If you wait for about 5 seconds and go back, the animation run with the timer function is already over, but the function run with the rAF() starts again from the spot.

rAF() performance optimization

window.addEventListener('scroll', function(e) {

  if (!ticking) {
    window.requestAnimationFrame(function() {
      doSomething();
      ticking = false;
    });

    ticking = true;
  }
});

I don't know where I saw it, but I saw a post saying that scroll events occur so much that I need to throttle them with the code above. Since then, I have applied the throttle unconditionally when using the scroll event, and when I happened to see the MDN docs, I realized that I had been mistaken.

Since the scroll event occurs when the scrolled screen is rendered, it is synchronized with the refresh rate. Therefore, because it runs at the same speed as rAF(), which is synchronized with the refresh rate, a throttle using rAF() is often unnecessary. So in what case is rAF() needed? It is necessary when using code that can cause layout thrashing as shown below.

function onScroll(){
  const box = element.getBoundingClientRect();
  if(box.top > pos){
    element.style.height = box.height * 2
  }
}

window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  onScroll()
});

Reflow, forced synchronous layout and layout thrashing

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/49550a29-3be5-43a4-a737-09c4dbdacb26.webp" alt="Rendering pipeline. source-https://web.dev/avoid-large-complex-layouts-and-layout-thrashing"}

The above figure shows the workflow when rendering, and it is difficult to explain all the steps, so let's just talk about the reflow required for today's topic.

reflow refers to the process of re-performing from the layout step when the geometric value of the DOM element is changed. As can be seen from the re-performance from the layout stage, it is a costly task because not only the changed elements but also the calculation of other elements must be re-performed. Therefore, the browser collects tasks that require reflow in the queue to reduce this cost and schedules them for processing in one reflow in the next frame.

However, when trying to process reading and writing geometric values in one frame, the reflow is performed immediately. This is called forced synchronous layout, and a phenomenon in which forced synchronous layout occurs repeatedly, resulting in performance degradation is called layout thrashing.

Prevent layout thrashing

The code mentioned above reads the geometric value of top, resulting in a forced synchronous layout. Therefore, we will use the features of rAF() described above to separate the read logic that causes the reflow and hand over the remaining computational logic to rAF() as a callback function. This effectively defends layout thrashing because the read is processed in the current frame, and the rest of the operations are collected in the next frame and processed in one time.

function onScroll(height){
  element.style.height = height * 2
}

window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  const box = element.getBoundingClientRect();
  if(box.top > pos){
      requestAnimationFrame(()=>{
          onScroll(box.height)
      })
  }
});

Test

Thankfully, someone has already made a testable example with this topic, so I tested it with the developer tool. It was processed at once to the extent that I wondered if this was right.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/45e67766-cbdd-4e93-8063-005cae1128ae.webp" alt="Without layout thrashing"}

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/0d3dbeff-76a0-4391-97fe-a14b3e10fdb5.webp" alt="With layout thrashing"}

Reference

  • MDN docs: requestAnimationFrame()
  • frame loss graph
  • requestAnimationFrame() and performance - Korean
  • Should rAF throttle be applied to scroll event - Korean
  • Front-end performance optimization: avoid layout thrashing with rAF - Korean
  • test example
