žčúžěĎŪēėÍłįžóź žēěžĄú, Ž™áŽ™á žö©žĖīŽ•ľ ŽįėŽ≥Ķ žā¨žö©Ūēīžēľ ŪēėŽäĒŽćį ÍłłžĖīžĄú ž∂ēžēĹ ž†ēžĚėŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŪēėÍ≤†Žč§.

  • setTimeout()žôÄ setInterval() => timer Ūē®žąė
  • requestAnimationFrame() => rAF()

CSSŽßĆžúľŽ°ú ŪĎúŪėĄŪēėÍłį žĖīŽ†§žöī žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖėžĚĄ ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŪēėÍłį žúĄŪēīžĄúŽäĒ žěźŽįĒžä§ŪĀ¨Ž¶ĹŪäłŽ•ľ žā¨žö©Ūēīžēľ ŪēėŽäĒŽćį, žĶúž†ĀŪôĒ žóÜžĚī žĚīŽ•ľ žā¨žö©ŪēėŽ©ī žĄĪŽä•žÉĀ Ž¨łž†úÍįÄ ŽįúžÉĚŪē† žąė žěąŽč§. rAF()ÍįÄ Žāėžė§Íłį ž†ĄžóźŽäĒ žĶúž†ĀŪôĒ ÍłįŽ≤ēžúľŽ°ú timer Ūē®žąėÍįÄ žā¨žö©ŽźźÍ≥†, Í∑ł žĚīŪõĄŽ°úŽäĒ rAF()žĚĄ žā¨žö©ŽźėÍ≥† žěąŽč§.

timer Ūē®žąė vs rAF()

žĚī žĄĻžÖėžóźžĄúŽäĒ timer Ūē®žąėžóź žĖīŽĖ§ Ž¨łž†úÍįÄ žěąžĖīžĄú rAF()ÍįÄ ŽāėžôĒŽäĒžßÄ ŽĻĄÍĶźŪēī Ž≥īŽ©į žēĆžēĄŽ≥īŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŪēėÍ≤†Žč§.

ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄ žú†žč§

timer Ūē®žąėŽäĒ ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžĚĄ Í≥†Ž†§ŪēėžßÄ žēäÍ≥† žěĎŽŹôŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź žĖłž†ú žĹúŽįĪ Ūē®žąėÍįÄ Ūėłž∂úŽźėŽäĒžßÄ Ž≥īžě•Ūē† žąė žóÜŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú žēĄŽěė Í∑łŽ¶ľž≤ėŽüľ ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžóź ÍĪłž≥źžĄú žč§ŪĖČŽźėŽ©ī ŪēīŽčĻ ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžĚĄ ŽÜďžĻėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖėžĚī Ž≤ĄŽ≤ÖÍĪįŽ¶¨Í≤Ć ŽźúŽč§.

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/54dfd618-50c9-463e-aa62-dd1919651fd6.webp" alt="ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄ žú†žč§ Í∑łŽěėŪĒĄ. ž∂úž†Ä - https://web.dev/optimize-javascript-execution/?hl=ko"}

::v-alert ūüí° Í∑łŽ¶ľžĚė 16msŽäĒ žĖīŽĖĽÍ≤Ć Žāėžė® ÍįížĚľÍĻĆ?
ŪäĻŽ≥ĄŪěą ŪôĒŽ©ī ž£ľžā¨žú®žĚĄ žĄ†ŪÉĚŪēīžĄú ÍĶ¨Žß§ŪēėžßÄ žēäžēėŽč§Ž©ī 60HzžĚė Ž™®ŽčąŪĄįŽ•ľ žā¨žö©ž§Ď žĚľ ŪÖźŽćį, žĚīŽäĒ 1žīąžóź 60ŪöĆ ŪôĒŽ©īžĚĄ ÍįĪžč†ŪēúŽč§ŽäĒ žĚėŽĮłžĚīŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú 1,000ms / 60ŪöĆ ‚Čą 16ms ÍįĄÍ≤©žúľŽ°ú ŪôĒŽ©īžĚĄ ÍįĪžč†ŪēėÍ≤Ć ŽźúŽč§. ::

ŽįėŽ©ī, rAF()ŽäĒ ŪôĒŽ©ī ž£ľžā¨žú®žóź ŽŹôÍłįŪôĒ ŽźėŽ©į, ŽłĆŽĚľžöįž†ÄÍįÄ ŪôĒŽ©īžĚĄ Í∑łŽ¶ī ž§ÄŽĻĄÍįÄ ŽźėžóąžĚĄ ŽēĆ žč§ŪĖČŽźúŽč§. ž°įÍłą ŽćĒ žěźžĄłŪěą ŽßźŪēėžěźŽ©ī, rAF()žóź žěĎžĄĪŪēú žĹúŽįĪŪē®žąėŽäĒ Animation Frame queuežóź ŽďĪŽ°ĚŽźėŽ©į, Žč§žĚĆ repaint žßĀž†Ąžóź žĚī ŪĀźžóź žěąŽäĒ Ž™®Žď† žĹúŽįĪŪē®žąėÍįÄ ŪēúŽ≤ąžóź ž≤ėŽ¶¨ŽźúŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú žúĄ Í∑łŽ¶ľž≤ėŽüľ žĹúŽįĪ Ūē®žąėÍįÄ žĖīž§ĎÍįĄŪēėÍ≤Ć žčúžěĎŽźėžĖīžĄú ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžĚī žú†žč§ŽźėŽäĒ Ž¨łž†úŽ•ľ Žį©žßÄŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

:v-iframe{ src="https://stackblitz.com/edit/web-platform-jujfzt?ctl=1&embed=1&file=example1.html" style="height: 560px" }

timer Ūē®žąėžôÄ rAF()Ž•ľ žĚīžö©ŪēīžĄú ÍįĀ Žįēžä§ÍįÄ žė§Ž•łž™ĹžúľŽ°ú 5pxžĒ©, žīĚ 1,000px žĚīŽŹôŪēėŽäĒ žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖėžĚĄ žėąž†ú žĹĒŽďúŽ°ú ŽßĆŽď§žóąŽč§. žēěžĄú žĖłÍłČŪĖąŽďĮžĚī žĖłž†ú žĹúŽįĪ Ūē®žąėÍįÄ žč§ŪĖČŽź†žßÄ Ž≥īžě•ŽźėžßÄ žēäÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź ŽąąžúľŽ°ú ŪôēžĚłŽź† ž†ēŽŹĄŽ°ú ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄ žú†žč§žĚī žĚľžĖīŽā† žąėŽŹĄ žěąÍ≥† Í∑łŽ†ážßÄ žēäžĚĄ žąėŽŹĄ žěąŽč§. žĚī Í≤Ĺžöį Ž™á žį®Ž°Ä ŽįėŽ≥ĶŪēėŽč§ Ž≥īŽ©ī ŪôēžĚłŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

žčúžä§ŪÖú Ž¶¨žÜĆžä§ Žā≠ŽĻĄ

timer Ūē®žąėŽäĒ žįĹžĚī ŽĻĄŪôúžĄĪŪôĒ žÉĀŪÉúžĚīÍĪįŽāė žą®Í≤®žßĄ iframe ŽāīžóźžĄúŽŹĄ ŽŹôžěĎŪēīžĄú žčúžä§ŪÖú Ž¶¨žÜĆžä§ÍįÄ Žā≠ŽĻĄŽźėžóąÍ≥†, žĚīžóź ŽĒįŽĚľ ŽįįŪĄįŽ¶¨ žąėŽ™ÖÍ≥ľ žĄĪŽä• ž†ÄŪēė Ž¨łž†úŽ•ľ žēľÍłįŪĖąŽč§. Í∑łŽü¨Žāė rAF()Ž•ľ žĚīžö©ŪēėŽ©ī žįĹžĚī ŽĻĄŪôúžĄĪŪôĒ žÉĀŪÉúÍįÄ ŽźźžĚĄ ŽēĆ žěĎžóÖžĚĄ ž§ĎžßÄŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź žčúžä§ŪÖú žěźžõźžĚĄ ž†ąžēĹŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

žĚī Žāīžö©ŽŹĄ žßĀž†Ď ŪôēžĚłŪēī Ž≥īÍłį žúĄŪēī žēěžĄú žĖłÍłČŪēú žėąž†ú žĹĒŽďúžĚė žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖėžĚĄ žč§ŪĖČŪēėžěźŽßąžěź ŽłĆŽĚľžöįž†ÄŽ•ľ Žč§Ž•ł ŪÉ≠žúľŽ°ú ž†ĄŪôėŪēī Ž≥īžěź. ŽĄČŽĄČŪěą 5žīą ž†ēŽŹĄ ÍłįŽč§Ž†łŽč§ÍįÄ ŽŹĆžēĄÍįÄ Ž≥īŽ©ī timer Ūē®žąėŽ°ú žč§ŪĖČŪēú žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖėžĚÄ žĚīŽĮł ŽĀĚŽāėžěąžßÄŽßĆ, rAF()Ž°ú žč§ŪĖČŪēú Ūē®žąėŽäĒ žēĄÍĻĆ Í∑ł žěźŽ¶¨žóźžĄúŽ∂ÄŪĄį Žč§žčú žčúžěĎŪēúŽč§.

rAF() žĄĪŽä• žĶúž†ĀŪôĒ

window.addEventListener('scroll', function(e) {

  if (!ticking) {
    window.requestAnimationFrame(function() {
      doSomething();
      ticking = false;
    });

    ticking = true;
  }
});

žĖīŽĒĒžĄú Žī§ŽäĒžßÄ Ž™®Ž•īÍ≤†ŽäĒŽćį, žä§ŪĀ¨Ž°§ žĚīŽ≤§ŪäłŽäĒ ŽĄąŽ¨ī ŽßéžĚī ŽįúžÉĚŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź žúĄžĚė žĹĒŽďúŽ°ú žä§Ž°úŪčÄ ž≤ėŽ¶¨Ž•ľ ŪēīžēľŽźúŽč§ŽäĒ ÍłÄžĚĄ Žī§Žč§. Í∑ł žĚīŪõĄŽ°ú žä§ŪĀ¨Ž°§ žĚīŽ≤§ŪäłŽ•ľ žā¨žö©Ūē† ŽēĆ Ž¨īž°įÍĪīž†ĀžúľŽ°ú žä§Ž°úŪčÄžĚĄ ž†Āžö©ŪĖąŽäĒŽćį žöįžóįŪěą MDN Ž¨łžĄúŽ•ľ Ž≥īŽčą, Í∑łŽŹôžēą žěėŽ™Ľ žēĆÍ≥† žěąžóąŽč§ŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚĄ ÍĻ®Žč≥žēėŽč§.

žä§ŪĀ¨Ž°§ žĚīŽ≤§ŪäłŽäĒ žä§ŪĀ¨Ž°§ Žźú ŪôĒŽ©īžĚĄ ŽěúŽćĒŽßĀ ŪĖąžĚĄ ŽēĆ ŽįúžÉĚŪēėŽĮÄŽ°ú ŪôĒŽ©ī ž£ľžā¨žú®Í≥ľ ŽŹôÍłįŪôĒ ŽźúŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú ŪôĒŽ©ī ž£ľžā¨žú®Í≥ľ ŽŹôÍłįŪôĒ ŽźėžĖīžěąŽäĒ rAF()žôÄ ÍįôžĚÄ žÜćŽŹĄŽ°ú žč§ŪĖČŽźėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź rAF()Ž•ľ žĚīžö©Ūēú žä§Ž°úŪčÄžĚÄ žĘÖžĘÖ Ž∂ąŪēĄžöĒŪēėŽč§. Í∑łŽ†áŽč§Ž©ī žĖīŽĖ§ Í≤Ĺžöįžóź rAF()ÍįÄ ŪēĄžöĒŪē†ÍĻĆ? ŽįĒŽ°ú, žēĄŽěėž≤ėŽüľ Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉ žä§Ž†ąžčĪžĚĄ žĚľžúľŪā¨ žąė žěąŽäĒ žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ žā¨žö©Ūē† ŽēĆ ŪēĄžöĒŪēėŽč§.

function onScroll(){
  const box = element.getBoundingClientRect();
  if(box.top > pos){
    element.style.height = box.height * 2
  }
}

window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  onScroll()
});

Reflow, Íįēž†ú ŽŹôÍłįžčĚ Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉ Í∑łŽ¶¨Í≥† Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉ žä§Ž†ąžčĪ

:v-prose-img{src="https://wbwsloxpaolxwerwiupa.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/blog-asset/images/49550a29-3be5-43a4-a737-09c4dbdacb26.webp" alt="ŽěúŽćĒŽßĀ ŪĆĆžĚīŪĒĄŽĚľžĚł. ž∂úž≤ė-https://web.dev/avoid-large-complex-layouts-and-layout-thrashing"}

žúĄ Í∑łŽ¶ľžĚÄ ŽěúŽćĒŽßĀ Ūē†ŽēĆ žěĎžóÖ ŪĚźŽ¶ĄžĚĄ ŽāėŪÉÄŽāīÍ≥† žěąŽäĒŽćį, ÍįĀ Žč®Í≥ĄŽ•ľ Ž™®ŽĎź žĄ§Ž™ÖŪēėÍłįžóźŽäĒ ŪĒľŽ°úŽŹĄÍįÄ ŽÜížúľŽĮÄŽ°ú žė§Žäė ž£ľž†úžóź ŪēĄžöĒŪēú reflowŽßĆ žßöÍ≥† ŽĄėžĖīÍįÄÍ≤†Žč§.

reflowŽäĒ DOM žöĒžÜĆžĚė ÍłįŪēėŪēôž†Ā ÍįížĚī Ž≥ÄÍ≤ĹŽźźžĚĄ ŽēĆ, layout Žč®Í≥ĄŽ∂ÄŪĄį žě¨žąėŪĖČŪēėŽäĒ Í≥ľž†ēžĚĄ ŽßźŪēúŽč§. layout Žč®Í≥ĄŽ∂ÄŪĄį žě¨žąėŪĖČŪēėŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚĄ ŪÜĶŪēī žēĆ žąė žěąŽďĮžĚī Ž≥ÄÍ≤ĹŪēú žöĒžÜĆŽŅźŽßĆ žēĄŽčąŽĚľ, Žč§Ž•ł žöĒžÜĆŽď§žĚė Í≥ĄžāįŽŹĄ žě¨žąėŪĖČŪēīžēľ ŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź ŽĻĄžö©žĚī ŪĀ¨Í≤Ć Žď§žĖīÍįÄŽäĒ žěĎžóÖžĚīŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú ŽłĆŽĚľžöįž†ÄŽäĒ žĚī ŽĻĄžö©žĚĄ ž§ĄžĚīÍłį žúĄŪēīžĄú reflowÍįÄ ŪēĄžöĒŪēú žěĎžóÖŽď§žĚĄ ŪĀźžóź Ž™®žēĄžĄú, Žč§žĚĆ ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžóź ŪēúŽ≤ąžĚė reflowŽ°ú ž≤ėŽ¶¨Ūē† žąė žěąŽŹĄŽ°Ě žėąžēĹžĚĄ ŪēúŽč§.

Í∑łŽü¨Žāė ÍłįŪēėŪēôž†Ā ÍįížĚĄ žĚĹÍ≥† žďįŽäĒ Í≤ÉžĚĄ Ūēú ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžóźžĄú ž≤ėŽ¶¨ŪēėŽ†§Í≥† Ūē† ŽēĆžóźŽäĒ ž¶Čžčú reflowŽ•ľ žąėŪĖČŪēėÍ≤Ć ŽźúŽč§. žĚīŽ•ľ Íįēž†ú ŽŹôÍłįžčĚ Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉžĚīŽĚľÍ≥† ŪēėŽ©į, Íįēž†ú ŽŹôÍłįžčĚ Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉžĚī ŽįėŽ≥Ķž†ĀžúľŽ°ú žĚľžĖīŽāė žĄĪŽä• ž†ÄŪēėŽ•ľ žīąŽěėŪēėŽäĒ ŪėĄžÉĀžĚĄ Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉ žä§Ž†ąžčĪžĚīŽĚľÍ≥† ŪēúŽč§.

Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉ žä§Ž†ąžčĪ Žį©žßÄ

žúĄžóźžĄú žĖłÍłČŪĖąŽćė žĹĒŽďúŽäĒ topžĚīŽĚľŽäĒ ÍłįŪēėŪēôž†ĀžĚł ÍįížĚĄ Ūēú ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžóźžĄú žĚĹÍ≥† žćľÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź Íįēž†ú ŽŹôÍłįžčĚ Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉžĚī ŽįúžÉĚŪēúŽč§. ŽĒįŽĚľžĄú žöįŽ¶¨ŽäĒ žēěžĄú žĄ§Ž™ÖŪēú rAF()žĚė ŪäĻžßēžĚĄ žĚīžö©ŪēīžĄú, reflowŽ•ľ žĚľžúľŪā§ŽäĒ žĚĹÍłį Ž°úžßĀžĚĄ Ž∂ĄŽ¶¨ŪēėÍ≥† ŽāėŽ®łžßÄ žóįžāį Ž°úžßĀžĚÄ rAF()žóź žĹúŽįĪŪē®žąėŽ°ú ŽĄėÍłįŽŹĄŽ°Ě Ūē† Í≤ÉžĚīŽč§. žĚīŽ†áÍ≤ĆŪēėŽ©ī žĚĹÍłįŽäĒ ŪėĄžě¨ ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžóźžĄú ž≤ėŽ¶¨ŪēėÍ≥†, ŽāėŽ®łžßÄ žóįžāįžĚÄ Žč§žĚĆ ŪĒĄŽ†ąžěĄžóź Ž™®žēĄžĄú Ūēú Ž≤ąžóź ž≤ėŽ¶¨ŽźėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź Ž†ąžĚīžēĄžõÉ žä§Ž†ąžčĪžĚĄ Ūö®Í≥ľž†ĀžúľŽ°ú Žį©žĖīŪē† žąė žěąŽč§.

function onScroll(height){
  element.style.height = height * 2
}

window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  const box = element.getBoundingClientRect();
  if(box.top > pos){
    requestAnimationFrame(()=>{
      onScroll(box.height)
    })
  }
});

ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł

Íįźžā¨ŪēėÍ≤ĆŽŹĄ žĚī Žāīžö©žúľŽ°ú ŽąĄÍĶįÍįÄ žĚīŽĮł ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ÍįÄŽä•Ūēú žėąž†úŽ•ľ ŽßĆŽď§žĖī ŽÜ®Íłįžóź ÍįúŽįúžěźŪąīŽ°ú ŪÖĆžä§Ūäł ŪēīŽī§Žč§. žĚīÍ≤Ć ŽßěŽäĒÍĪīÍįÄ žč∂žĚĄ ž†ēŽŹĄŽ°ú ŪēúŽ≤ąžóź ž≤ėŽ¶¨ŽźźŽč§.

  • ŪĒĄŽ°†ŪäłžóĒŽďú žĄĪŽä• žĶúž†ĀŪôĒ : layout thrashing ŪĒľŪēėÍłį with requestAnimationFrame

item.title: How to measure execution time in Java/Kotlin. +Spring Framework
item.description: System.currentTimeMillis() and System.nanoTime() are widely known as popular methods for measuring run time in Java. In this article, we will measu...
item.content:encoded:
System.currentTimeMillis() and System.nanoTime() are widely known as popular methods for measuring run time in Java. In this article, we will measure the execution time using the latter for several reasons, and we will briefly summarize the reason.

Why System.nanoTime()?

First, as the name suggests, it can be measured up to nanoseconds, so it can be measured 106 times more precisely.

Second, if you read Java doc, you can see the explanation, This method can only be used to measure elapsed time and is not related to any other notion of system or wall-clock time.. As can be seen from the description, this method was created from the beginning to measure elapsed time.

Third, System.currentTimeMillis() returns wall-clock time, so inaccurate values can be measured by external variables such as OS time changes or leap seconds.

Java

long start = System.nanoTime();
// Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(500);
long finish = System.nanoTime();
// via milliseconds
long elapsedTime = (finish - start) / 1_000_000;
System.out.printf("elapsed = %sms" , elapsedTime);

elapsed = 505ms

Kotlin

You can use Java code as it is, but let's use it because it provides a util method that makes it easier to measure execution time in Kotlin.

measureTimeMillis()

The execution time can be measured in milliseconds.

import kotlin.system.measureTimeMillis

val elapsed = measureTimeMillis {
  // Heavy task simulation
  Thread.sleep(500)
}
println("elapsed = ${elapsed}ms")

elapsed = 507ms

measureNanoTime()

The execution time can be measured in nanoseconds.

import kotlin.system.measureNanoTime

val elapsed = measureNanoTime {
  // Heavy task simulation
  Thread.sleep(500)
}
println("elapsed = ${elapsed}ns")

elapsed = 504509000ns

measureTime()

Returns the Duration object whose value has been converted from nanoseconds to milliseconds.

import kotlin.time.measureTime

val elapsed = measureTime {
  // Heavy task simulation
  Thread.sleep(500)
}
println("elapsed = $elapsed")

elapsed = 505.351542ms

measureTimedValue()

Returns the TimedValue data class consisting of measurement time and in-block return values.

import kotlin.time.measureTimedValue

val timedValue = measureTimedValue {
  // Heavy task simulation
  Thread.sleep(500)
  "Hello world"
}
println("timedValue = $timedValue")

timedValue = TimedValue(value=Hello world, duration=507.327875ms)

Spring framework

Get total time 

import org.springframework.util.StopWatch;

StopWatch sw = new StopWatch();

sw.start();
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(200);
sw.stop();

sw.start();
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(500);
sw.stop();

sw.start();
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(1000);
sw.stop();

System.out.printf("elapsed = %sms" , sw.getTotalTimeMillis());

elapsed = 1714ms

Get last task time 

import org.springframework.util.StopWatch;

StopWatch sw = new StopWatch();

sw.start();
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(200);
sw.stop();

sw.start();
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(500);
sw.stop();

sw.start();
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(1000);
sw.stop();

System.out.printf("elapsed = %sms" , sw.getLastTaskTimeMillis());

elapsed = 1005ms

Get summary by task name 

import org.springframework.util.StopWatch;

StopWatch sw = new StopWatch();

sw.start("A");//setting a task name
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(200);
sw.stop();

sw.start("B");//setting a task name
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(500);
sw.stop();

sw.start("C");//setting a task name
//Heavy task simulation
Thread.sleep(1000);
sw.stop();

System.out.println(sw);
System.out.println();
System.out.println(sw.prettyPrint());

StopWatch '': running time = 1711237667 ns; [A] took 205087208 ns = 12%; [B] took 505058167 ns = 30%; [C] took 1001092292 ns = 59%

StopWatch '': running time = 1711237667 ns
---------------------------------------------
ns         %     Task name
---------------------------------------------
205087208  012%  A
505058167  030%  B
1001092292  059%  C

ŽĎėžßłŽ°ú, Java docžĚĄ žĚĹžĖīŽ≥īŽ©ī This method can only be used to measure elapsed time and is not related to any other notion of system or wall-clock time. ŽĚľŽäĒ žĄ§Ž™ÖžĚĄ Ž≥ľ žąė žěąŽč§. žĄ§Ž™ÖžĚĄ ŪÜĶŪēī žēĆ žąė žěąŽďĮžĚī, žĚī Ž©ĒžĄúŽďúŽäĒ žē†žīąŽ∂ÄŪĄį Í≤ĹÍ≥ľžčúÍįĄ žł°ž†ēžĚĄ žúĄŪēī ŽßĆŽď§žĖīžßĄ Ž©ĒžĄúŽďúžĚīŽč§.

item.title: How to git clone only a specific directory
item.description: Sometimes, only specific directories are needed in the git repository, and at this time, the `Sparse Checkout` function added from the V1.7 can be...
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes, only specific directories are needed in the git repository, and at this time, the Sparse checkout added from the v1.7 can be used.

Let's Give it a shot

For a smooth understanding, we have prepared files in the https://github.com/besfir/tutorial-sparse.git repository, and the structure is as follows.

‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ .gitignore
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ content
    ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ dev
    ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ child

    ‚Ēā   ‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ file_2.md           ‚úÖ

In this repository, we will try to replicate only the content/dev/child and content/hobby directories of the main branch. If you have 5 files that you ticked when you completed the final stage, it is completed normally.

Initialize the local repository to which the file will be cloned

git init tutorial-sparse
cd tutorial-sparse

Enable sparse checkout config

git config core.sparseCheckout true

Define remotes

git remote add -f origin https://github.com/besfir/tutorial-sparse.git

Specify the directory you want to clone

echo "content/dev/child" >> .git/info/sparse-checkout
echo "content/hobby" >> .git/info/sparse-checkout

Pull repository

git pull origin main

After pulling the repository, I tried printing the structure with the tree command.

item.title: Setting the timezone within Alpine linux docker image
item.description: In a production environment, a time zone must be manually set up for reasons such as cron job, log time localization, and time synchronization betw...
item.content:encoded:
In a production environment, it may be necessary to set a time zone manually for reasons such as cron job, log time localization, and time synchronization between DB and OS. The method of setting the time zone varies depending on the linux distribution, and in this article, we will try how to set it up in Alpine linux, which is often used to build a docker environment, and how to optimize it in docker.

Set up timezone in Alpine linux

Install tzdata package

Alpine Linux does not include packages related to time zone settings. Therefore, the tzdata package must be installed first.

apk add tzdata

(Optional) I checked with the 'ls' command to see if it was installed properly.

/app # ls -L /usr/share/zoneinfo/
Africa             CST6CDT            Etc                Greenwich          Kwajalein          PRC                UCT                posixrules
America            Canada             Europe             HST                Libya              PST8PDT            US                 right
Antarctica         Chile              Factory            Hongkong           MET                Pacific            UTC                zone.tab
Arctic             Cuba               GB                 Iceland            MST                Poland             Universal          zone1970.tab
Asia               EET                GB-Eire            Indian             MST7MDT            Portugal           W-SU
Atlantic           EST                GMT                Iran               Mexico             ROC                WET
Australia          EST5EDT            GMT+0              Israel             NZ                 ROK                Zulu
Brazil             Egypt              GMT-0              Jamaica            NZ-CHAT            Singapore          iso3166.tab
CET                Eire               GMT0               Japan              Navajo             Turkey             leap-seconds.list

Copy the proper zone info to localtime

cp /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime

Specify timezone

echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone

Check the datetime with date command

date

Fri May 12 12:44:46 KST 2023

Optimize docker image

When setting timezone in Docker, slightly deform the code mentioned above. When installing a package, use the --no-cache flag to disable cache, and perform capacity optimization by deleting packages that are no longer needed.

FROM node:18.16.0-alpine3.17  

RUN apk add --no-cache tzdata && \
    cp /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime && \
    echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone && \
    apk del tzdata

(Optional) With multi-stage builds

::v-alert Multi-stage builds is a feature supported since docker v17.06, and is a step-by-step function to exclude elements that are necessary for building docker images but not necessary for the final result image. ::

When docker files become complicated, removing files that are not needed for result can be daunting. If you use multi-stage buildings, you can focus only on the resources you need.

FROM node:18.16.0-alpine3.17 AS base

# Builder process
FROM base AS builder

RUN apk add tzdata

# Runner process
FROM base AS runner

COPY --from=builder /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime
RUN echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone

Optimize docker image

ŽŹĄžĽ§žóźžĄú timezonežĚĄ žĄ§ž†ēŪē† ŽēĆŽäĒ žúĄžóźžĄú žĖłÍłČŪēú žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ žēĹÍįĄ Ž≥ÄŪėēžčúŪā®Žč§. ŪĆ®Ūā§žßÄŽ•ľ žĄ§žĻėŪē† ŽēĆ žļźžčúŽ•ľ žā¨žö©ŪēėžßÄ žēäÍłį žúĄŪēī --no-cache ŪĒĆŽěėÍ∑łŽ•ľ žā¨žö©ŪēėÍ≥†, ŽćĒ žĚīžÉĀ ŪēĄžöĒžĻė žēäžĚÄ ŪĆ®Ūā§žßÄŽ•ľ žā≠ž†úŪēėžó¨ žö©ŽüČ žĶúž†ĀŪôĒŽ•ľ žąėŪĖČŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŪēúŽč§.

item.title: Make animated number counter with Vanilla js. +Vue3
item.description: I implemented it because I needed to use a animated number counter, but when I saw my code that I implemented, I was very annoyed. Becuase my code...
item.content:encoded:
I implemented it because I needed to use a animated number counter, but when I saw my code that I implemented, I was very annoyed. Becuase my code didn't make sense. So I searched for code made by someone else and managed to find code that satisfies the requirements that can be duration-specified and can display commas every 3 digits, and leave a record.

Code

:v-iframe{ src="https://stackblitz.com/edit/js-vl7vcq?ctl=1&embed=1&file=index.js" }

How it works

To put it really simply, the counter we created increases in numbers at the same rate from start to finish, so we just have to calculate the number increased by the elapsed time and repeat it over and over again until we finish.

To explain it in more detail, if you multiply timeLapseRate, which is the ratio of elapsed to duration, by range(range that to animate the number), and add start (initial value), you can obtain the value that should be shown at the elapsed time. By repeating these tasks by the recursive function during duration, we can get the animation effect we want.

It's hard just to say it, so let's say you make an animation that counts from 20 to 100 for 4 seconds.

  • duration = 4, start = 20, range = 80
  • At the end of one second, elapsed = 1, so the value we see is 1/4*80+20 = 40.
  • After 2 seconds, it becomes 2/4*80+20 = 60.
  • After 3 seconds, it becomes 3/4*80+20 = 80.
  • After 4 seconds, 4/4*80+20 = 100.

Explanation

Let's look at how the operating principles described above are applied in the code.

const duration = 1000;

const counter = (el) => {
  const start = +el.innerText;
  const end = +el.dataset.counter;

  if (start === end) {
    return;
  }

  const range = end - start;
  let currentValue = start;

  const timeStart = Date.now();

  const loop = () => {
    const elapsed = Date.now() - timeStart;
    const timeLapseRate = Math.min(elapsed / duration, 1);      // (2)
    const change = timeLapseRate * range;
    currentValue = start + change;
    el.innerText = Math.trunc(currentValue).toLocaleString();   // (3)
    if (timeLapseRate === 1) {                                  // (1)
      return;
    }
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);                                // (4)
  };

  requestAnimationFrame(loop);
};

(1) When 'timeLapseRate' becomes 1, that is, when time elapses by the duration we specify, this recursive function ends.

(2) Using requestAnimationFrame, elapsed goes beyond duration and timeLapseRate may be greater than 1. In order to prevent this and make calculations easier, make the result value not exceed 1.

(3) Math.trunc() was used to achieve the same effect with negative numbers. In addition, in order to respond to the number format, toLocaleString() was used to show it in a number format suitable for each browser language. In my case, since i set Korean as the browser language, the number format displays a comma every three digits.

(4) Repeat this task to provide animation effects.

Vue3 Code

I wrote the Vue3 code with the same mechanism because I use Vue for front-end work.

:v-iframe{ src="https://stackblitz.com/edit/vitejs-vite-minwal?ctl=1&embed=1&file=src/components/TheCounter.vue" }

item.title: Vanilla jsŽ°ú žąęžěź žĻīžöīŪäł žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖė ŽßĆŽď§Íłį. +Vue3
item.description: žąęžěź žĻīžöīŪĆÖ žē†ŽčąŽ©ĒžĚīžÖėžĚĄ žā¨žö©Ūē† ŪēĄžöĒÍįÄ žěąžĖīžĄú žĚīŽ•ľ ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŪĖąŽäĒŽćį, ŽßČžÉĀ ÍĶ¨ŪėĄŪēú Žāī žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ Ž≥īŽčą Ž™Ļžčú žßúž¶ĚžĚī ŽįÄŽ†§žôĒŽč§. item.content:encoded:

žúĄžóźžĄú žĄ§Ž™ÖŪēú ŽŹôžěĎ žõźŽ¶¨ÍįÄ žĹĒŽďúžóźžĄú žĖīŽĖĽÍ≤Ć ž†Āžö©ŽźėžóąŽäĒžßÄ žāīŪéīŽ≥īžěź. 

const duration = 1000;

const counter = (el) => {
  const start = +el.innerText;
  const end = +el.dataset.counter;

  if (start === end) {
    return;
  }

  const range = end - start;
  let currentValue = start;

  const timeStart = Date.now();

  const loop = () => {
    const elapsed = Date.now() - timeStart;
    const timeLapseRate = Math.min(elapsed / duration, 1);      // (2)
    const change = timeLapseRate * range;
    currentValue = start + change;
    el.innerText = Math.trunc(currentValue).toLocaleString();   // (3)
    if (timeLapseRate === 1) {                                  // (1)
      return;
    }
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);                                // (4)
  };

  requestAnimationFrame(loop);
};

item.title: Can i use PM2 inside Docker?
item.description: I recently re-checked the project I created when I didn't know Docker at all and found that I was using PM2 inside Docker. It didn't fit the best p...
item.content:encoded:
I recently re-checked the project I created when I didn't know Docker at all and found that I was using PM2 inside Docker. It didn't fit the best practices mentioned in the Docker document, but I thought it might be an exception, so I investigated it.

Monitoring becomes difficult.

Since PM2 takes charge of the task that Docker was doing, it becomes difficult to monitor CPU/Memory metrics in Docker. In addition, even if the service dies, it will not be known from Docker because it will restart in PM2. Therefore, normal monitoring cannot be performed in the monitoring system (Cloudwatch, etc.).

It goes against Docker's best practice.

As a best practice mentioned in the official document, it is recommended to use one service per container for separation of concern. Therefore, using PM2 internally for clustering is not a very good example.

See Why is it recommended to run only one process in a container?

Docker and PM2 are interchangeable.

The main purposes of using PM2 include clustering, abnormally terminated process restart, and logging. This can be replaced to some extent in Docker, where clustering can be replaced by docker-compose's scale command, abnormally terminated process restart can be replaced by restart policy, and logging can be replaced by logs command. Therefore, it would be better to choose and use it according to the situation rather than using more computing resources by using both.

Conclusion

If you check the PM2 document Docker Integration, it will be possible to implement it. However, based on the above, I found that using PM2 inside Docker does more harm than good. Therefore, I will conclude that it is better not to use PM2 inside Docker and conclude the article.

Reference

  • what is the point of using pm2 and docker together?
  • Top 4 Tactics To Keep Node.js Rockin‚Äô in Docker
  • Run multiple services in a container

item.title: Why is it recommended to run only one process in a container?
item.description: In the Docker document recommends the use of one service per container for separation of areas of interest as a best practice. In addition, many do...
item.content:encoded:
It‚Äôs best practice to separate areas of concern by using one service per container.

Docker documentation

In the Docker document recommends the use of one service per container for separation of areas of interest as a best practice. In addition, many documents online mention this a lot, so why encourage the use of one service per container?

Benefits of running a single service per container

  • Scalability: It makes it much easier to scale specific services as needed.
    If multi-services are operated in one container, even the same service may require different settings for each container when horizontally expanding. In this case, it may be more complicated than when the docker is not used.
  • Reusability: The service becomes much easier to reuse in other projects that require it.
    If multi-services were run in one container, they would not be available in other projects because they are specialized in the project.
  • Portability and Predictability: Since the scope of work reduced, it becomes much easier to debug security patches or errors.
    If multi-services are operated in one container, other non-patch-related services in the container may also be affected when applying security patches due to the high degree of combination between services. Therefore, it may be necessary to perform tasks that would not have been necessary for a single service per container configuration. This means that portability is low, and predictability is also low because it is relatively difficult to predict the range of effects of errors.

Disadvantages of running multi-service per container

  • Log processing: If multi-services are run in one container, it is difficult to identify which service is output from. Therefore, additional settings should be made to enable identification, or logs should be redirected to a separate file.
  • Monitoring: Docker performs container health checks with PID 1. Therefore, if PID 1 is normal when driving multi-services, it may not be detected even if the rest of the process is terminated due to an error.

Conclusion

It was confirmed that it was better to configure a container with a single service due to the advantages of single service per container + the disadvantages of multi-service per container. One thing that I regret is that I wanted to check what would happen if there was an exception, but I couldn't record it because there were no significant examples. If I find a good example later, I will add it.

Reference

  • Containers: one single process, or multiple processes?
  • Docker best practice:
  • Why it is recommended to run only one process in a container?

item.title: Create a custom function similar to timeago.js using Intl RelativeTimeFormat.
item.description: If you look at Instagram, Twitter, and YouTube, the time when the content was uploaded in the form of relative time such as xx seconds ago and xx m...
item.content:encoded:
When you look at Instagram, Twitter, and YouTube, the time when the content was uploaded in the form of relative time such as 'xx seconds ago' and 'xx minutes ago' is displayed. It can be easily implemented using some libraries.

However, since it can be implemented easily and similarly through the JS built-in API Intl, I would like to take this opportunity to record the practice.

Code

:v-iframe{ src="https://stackblitz.com/edit/js-hkoqei?ctl=1&embed=1&file=index.js" }

Explanation

It was declared in advance by pairing the unit that wanted to be expressed as the relative time with the value converted into ms units.

const units = {
    year: 31_536_000_000,   // 864_000_000(day) * 365
    month: 2_629_800_000,   // 31_536_000_000(year) / 12
    week: 604_800_000,      // 86_400_000(day) * 7 
    day: 86_400_000,
    hour: 3_600_000,
    minute: 60_000,
    second: 1_000,
};

Cycle the units specified above sequentially to verify that the diff value is divided by 1 or more. If zero comes out, it does not correspond to that unit, so it goes to the next step.

ex) diff = 120_123, amount = 2, unit = 'minute' 

function formatRelative(when, locale = 'en') {
    const formatter = new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat(locale, {
        numeric: "auto",
    });

    const diff = when - Date.now();
    for (const [unit, value] of Object.entries(units)) {
        const amount = Math.trunc(diff / value);        //(1)
        if (amount || unit === 'second') {              //(2)
            return formatter.format(amount, unit);
        }
    }
}

(1) Math.trunc() was used to cut the decimal point. Due to the expression structure, -1123ms is marked 1 second before and 1123ms is marked 1 second after, so the decimal point cut was required because to make number marked close to zero.

(2) Values less than 1 second are filtered by unit==='second' expression and now returned.

item.content:encoded:

žĹĒŽďú

:v-iframe{ src="https://stackblitz.com/edit/js-hkoqei?ctl=1&embed=1&file=index.js" }

ŪēīžĄ§

žÉĀŽĆÄ žčúÍįĄžúľŽ°ú ŪĎúŪėĄŪēėÍ≥†žěź ŪēėŽäĒ Žč®žúĄžôÄ ms Žč®žúĄŽ°ú ŪôėžāįŪēú ÍįížĚĄ žßĚžßÄžĖī ŽĮłŽ¶¨ žĄ†žĖłŪĖąŽč§.

const units = {
    year: 31_536_000_000,   // 864_000_000(day) * 365
    month: 2_629_800_000,   // 31_536_000_000(year) / 12
    week: 604_800_000,      // 86_400_000(day) * 7 
    day: 86_400_000,
    hour: 3_600_000,
    minute: 60_000,
    second: 1_000,
}

Ž™®Žď† Žč®žúĄŽ•ľ žąúŪöĆŪēėŽ©īžĄú ÍįĀ Žč®žúĄÍįÄ 1 žĚīžÉĀžúľŽ°ú ŽāėŽą†žßÄŽäĒžßÄ ŪôēžĚłŪēúŽč§. 0 žĚīžÉĀžĚī Žāėžė®Žč§Ž©ī, Í∑ł Žč®žúĄžóź ŪēīŽčĻŪēėžßÄ žēäžúľŽĮÄŽ°ú Žč§žĚĆ Žč®žúĄŽ°ú ŽĄėžĖīÍįĄŽč§.

ex) diff = 120_123, amount = 2, unit = 'minute' 

function formatRelative(when, locale = 'en') {
    const formatter = new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat(locale, {
        numeric: "auto",
    });

    const diff = when - Date.now();
    for (const [unit, value] of Object.entries(units)) {
        const amount = Math.trunc(diff / value);        //(1)
        if (amount || unit === 'second') {              //(2)
            return formatter.format(amount, unit);
        }

}

(1) žÜĆžąėž†źžĚĄ ž†ąžā≠ŪēėÍłį žúĄŪēī Math.trunc()Ž•ľ žā¨žö©ŪĖąŽč§. ŪĎúŪėĄ ÍĶ¨ž°įžÉĀ -1123msŽäĒ 1žīą ž†ĄžúľŽ°ú, 1123msŽäĒ 1žīą ŪõĄŽ°ú ŪĎúÍłįŽźėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź 0žóź ÍįÄÍĻĚŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŪĎúÍłįŪēīžēľ ŪēėŽĮÄŽ°ú žÜĆžąėž†źžĚĄ ž†ąžā≠žĚī ŪēĄžöĒŪĖąŽč§.

(2) 1žīą ŽĮłŽßĆžĚė ÍįíŽď§žĚÄ unit==='second'žóźžĄú ÍĪłŽü¨ž†łžĄú žßÄÍłąžĚĄ ŽįėŪôėŪēúŽč§.

item.title: Troubleshooting the inability to switch to the default locale on the Nuxti18n root path.
item.description: While working with Nuxt-i18n v8.0.0-beta.10, it was discovered that the `switchLocalePath()` function could not be used to move to the root path. T...
item.content:encoded:
Background

While working with Nuxt-i18n v8.0.0-beta.10, it was discovered that the switchLocalePath() function could not be used to move to the root path. There was no problem with routing between other routes, and only routing through locale switching from the root path was problematic. :v-youtube{ id="goY9WcUOB5E" title="Trouble demo video" }

Problem solving process

Narrowing the problem

The problem was expected in the browser language detection settings because the problem occurs only in the root path. This is because there is a part in which the root path is explicitly related. As expected, when I removed the most suspicious alwaysRedirect: true option, the locale transition worked normally.

i18n: {
  baseUrl: config.baseUrl,
  strategy: "prefix_except_default",
  defaultLocale: "en",
  detectBrowserLanguage: {
    useCookie: true,
    cookieKey: "i18n_redirected",
    redirectOn: "root",
    alwaysRedirect: true,
    fallbackLocale: "en",
  },
},

Temporary solution

I know the cause, but I couldn't think of a solution, so I used it as a prefix strategy for a while. Using this strategy, the url path only needs /en <==> /ko switching, so the settings described in the previous step did not effect it, so we decided to apply it temporarily until we found an alternative.

Searching

Previous step solution was worked, but it wasn't the way I wanted it to be, and it seemed more natural to use prefix exception default strategy, so I wanted to apply it somehow. Therefore, I had to solve the problem by searching, but it is really difficult to set the search keyword when there is an uncommon problem like this problem.

Eventually I couldn't decide on the search keyword and started looking thoughtlessly for similar issues on Nuxt-i18n Github, fortunately I was able to find the Github issue I wanted without taking long.

Solution

I was able to solve the problem with Bobby Goede's code. However, I prefer simpler code if it works the same function, so I decided to handle the click event directly without expanding the nuxt-link.

Code

I've extracted some of the codes I'm currently using.

<script setup lang="ts">
import { LocaleObject } from "vue-i18n-routing"

const { locale, locales } = useI18n()
const switchLocalePath = useSwitchLocalePath()

const availableLocales = computed(() => {
  return (locales.value as LocaleObject[]).filter(i => i.code !== locale.value)
})

const router = useRouter()
const cookie = useCookie("i18n_redirected")
const onClickLocaleSwitch = (code: string) => {
  cookie.value = code
  router.push(switchLocalePath(code))
}
</script>

<template>
  <nuxt-link
    v-for="locale in availableLocales"
    :key="locale.code"
    :to="switchLocalePath(locale.code)"
    @click.prevent="onClickLocaleSwitch(locale.code)"
  >
    <icon name="mdi:translate" size="20" class="mr-1" />
    {{ locale.name }}
  </nuxt-link>
</template>

modules: ['@nuxtjs/i18n'],
i18n: {
  baseUrl: config.baseUrl,
  langDir: "./helpers/locales",
  locales: [
    { code: "ko", iso: "ko-KR", name: "ŪēúÍĶ≠žĖī", file: "ko.json" },
    { code: "en", iso: "en-US", name: "English", file: "en.json" },
  ],
  strategy: "prefix_except_default",
  defaultLocale: "en",
  detectBrowserLanguage: {
  useCookie: true,
    cookieKey: "i18n_redirected",
    redirectOn: "root",
    alwaysRedirect: true,
    fallbackLocale: "en",
  },
},

BobbieGoedežĚė žĹĒŽďúŽ°ú Ž¨łž†úŽ•ľ ŪēīÍ≤įŪē† žąė žěąžóąŽč§. Žč§ŽßĆ, ÍįôžĚÄ ÍłįŽä•žĚĄ ŪēúŽč§Ž©ī ŽćĒ ÍįĄŽč®Ūēú žĹĒŽďúŽ•ľ žĄ†ŪėłŪēėÍłį ŽēĆŽ¨łžóź nuxt-linkŽ•ľ Ūôēžě•ŪēėžßÄ žēäÍ≥† ŪĀīŽ¶≠ žĚīŽ≤§ŪäłŽ•ľ žßĀž†Ď ž≤ėŽ¶¨ŪēėŽŹĄŽ°Ě ŪĖąŽč§.

Code

ŪėĄžě¨ žā¨žö©ž§ĎžĚł žĹĒŽďú žĚľŽ∂ÄŽ•ľ ÍįÄž†łžôĒŽč§.

<script setup lang="ts">
import { LocaleObject } from "vue-i18n-routing"

const { locale, locales } = useI18n()
const switchLocalePath = useSwitchLocalePath()

const availableLocales = computed(() => {
  return (locales.value as LocaleObject[]).filter(i => i.code !== locale.value)
})

const router = useRouter()
const cookie = useCookie("i18n_redirected")
const onClickLocaleSwitch = (code: string) => {
  cookie.value = code
  router.push(switchLocalePath(code))
}
</script>

<template>
  <nuxt-link
    v-for="locale in availableLocales"
    :key="locale.code"
    :to="switchLocalePath(locale.code)"
    @click.prevent="onClickLocaleSwitch(locale.code)"
  >
    <icon name="mdi:translate" size="20" class="mr-1" />
    {{ locale.name }}
  </nuxt-link>
</template>

modules: ['@nuxtjs/i18n'],
i18n: {
  baseUrl: config.baseUrl,
  langDir: "./helpers/locales",
  locales: [
    { code: "ko", iso: "ko-KR", name: "ŪēúÍĶ≠žĖī", file: "ko.json" },
    { code: "en", iso: "en-US", name: "English", file: "en.json" },
  ],
  strategy: "prefix_except_default",
  defaultLocale: "en",
  detectBrowserLanguage: {
  useCookie: true,
    cookieKey: "i18n_redirected",
    redirectOn: "root",
    alwaysRedirect: true,
    fallbackLocale: "en",
  },
},
